section1.title: What Is Heartopia?
section1.content: Heartopia is your cozy, 3D open-world sanctuary where the pace of life is entirely up to you. Developed as a next-generation life simulator, it blends the charm of Animal Crossing with a vibrant, modern social twist. In this world of soft macaron hues and gentle sunrises, you aren't just playing a game—you're crafting a second life. Whether you’re a collector, a decorator, or a social butterfly, Heartopia offers a boundless canvas for your imagination.

section2.title: How Do You Play Heartopia?
section2.content: There is no "right way" to play, only "your way." Dive into a variety of "Hobby" systems designed to reward your curiosity:

hobbies.title: Explore & Collect
hobbies.desc: Grab your rod for Heartopia fishing to find rare Seahorses, or roam the woods for Rare Timber and hidden insects.

hobbies.title: Master the Crafts
hobbies.desc: Follow the Mushroom Pie recipe in your cozy kitchen, or use our Blueprints to build the architectural masterpiece of your dreams.

hobbies.title: Socialize & Party
hobbies.desc: Join musical jams with neighbors or host a home party in your custom-decorated haven.

hobbies.title: Express Yourself
hobbies.desc: From changing your hair style to curated 1000+ daily outfits, your avatar is your ultimate self-expression.

section3.title: What Makes Heartopia Appealing?
section3.content: Healing at its Core. In a world that’s often too loud, Heartopia is a quiet "Deep Breath." It appeals to the soul through:

points.title: The Visual Hug
points.desc: A soft, rounded 3D aesthetic and a soothing color palette that reduces stress the moment you log in.

points.title: Pressure-Free Progression
points.desc: No daily chores that feel like work. You fish when you want, and sleep when you want.

points.title: Meaningful Connections
points.desc: It’s a "Slow Social" experience. Meet characters like Doris, Vanya, and Blanc, or bond with real players through shared hobbies and shared sunsets.

points.title: The Joy of Discovery
points.desc: That thrill when a Rainbow appears after the rain, signaling a world of rare finds.

faq.title: Heartopia Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our Heartopia guide and the world of Heartopia.
faq.q1: What is this Heartopia website?
faq.a1: This is an official-style companion guide created to help players understand systems and daily life in Heartopia without breaking immersion.
faq.q2: Is this a complete Heartopia walkthrough?
faq.a2: No. This Heartopia guide focuses on explaining how systems work rather than providing step-by-step completion paths.
faq.q3: Does this site include Heartopia fishing and fish locations?
faq.a3: Yes. Heartopia fishing, Heartopia fish, and Heartopia fish locations are covered in detail, with attention to time, weather, and environment.
faq.q4: Why does information sometimes differ from my game?
faq.a4: Heartopia systems may vary by updates, events, or player progress. This guide reflects general behavior rather than fixed outcomes.
faq.q5: Is this guide suitable for new players?
faq.a5: Yes. This Heartopia guide is designed to support both new and experienced players without overwhelming either group.
faq.q6: How do I find specific fish like the Seahorse or rare insects?
faq.a6: Our guide uses a Real-time Map & Spawn Tracker. Simply visit our Fishing Guide or Insect Map, and we’ll show you the exact coordinates based on the current in-game weather and time. No more aimless wandering!
faq.q7: Where can I get the latest Heartopia Blueprints and House Ideas?
faq.a7: We have a dedicated Community Gallery and Blueprint Database. You can filter by style (Cozy, Modern, Fairy-tale) and see exactly which materials, like Roaming Oak Timber, are needed for each build.
faq.q8: Is this site officially affiliated with XD Inc.?
faq.a8: We are a fan-made community hub created by passionate Heartopia players. Our goal is to provide the most accurate, visual-rich data that the official site might not cover, such as hidden recipes and gift preferences for NPCs like Doris and Blanc.

fishingGuide.metaTitle: Master Fishing in Heartopia: Complete Guide to All 94 Species
fishingGuide.metaDesc: Your essential Heartopia fishing companion: discover how to unlock fishing through Vanya's quest, understand line mechanics, leverage weather patterns for rare catches, and progress through all 10 levels.
fishingGuide.heroDesc: Dive into Heartopia's aquatic world with our comprehensive fishing tutorial. From your first cast with Vanya to mastering all 94 species, we'll show you how weather conditions, bait selection, and location knowledge combine to create the ultimate angling experience.
fishingGuide.quote: When you spot that rainbow in your weather forecast, drop everything—those are your golden hours for landing the rarest and most valuable fish in the game.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.unlockIntro: Your fishing journey begins when you encounter Vanya during the early tutorial sequence.
gettingStarted.unlock1: Continue the story until Vanya requests your assistance with their equipment.
gettingStarted.unlock2: Create a Repair Kit and deliver it to Vanya to help fix their fishing rod.
gettingStarted.unlock3: As thanks, Vanya grants you a Hobby Expansion Ticket along with your very own fishing rod.
gettingStarted.step1: Select your rod from the toolbox, then scan the water for dark silhouettes—larger shadows typically indicate more valuable catches.
gettingStarted.step2: Position yourself toward the target, press the rod button, then control your casting distance by timing your release.
gettingStarted.step3: Watch for your bobber to vanish beneath the surface—an exclamation mark signals a successful hook.
gettingStarted.step4: Press and hold the reel button while monitoring the line's color indicator; letting go when it flashes red prevents line breaks.
gettingStarted.lineWhite: White indicator — reel freely without risk.
gettingStarted.lineRed: Red indicator — stop reeling now or lose your catch.

whatIsFishing.title: Understanding Heartopia's Fishing System
whatIsFishing.p1: Your success depends on three interconnected factors: where you cast, when you fish, and what the weather's doing. Calm waters like lakes offer gentler learning curves for newcomers, while flowing rivers and vast ocean zones challenge experienced anglers with exclusive species.
whatIsFishing.p2: Track your collection progress through your phone's Fish Observation feature under Collections. Your smartwatch becomes invaluable for planning—its weather predictions highlight rainbow conditions, which dramatically boost your odds of encountering premium species.
whatIsFishing.p3: Beyond simple collection, fishing integrates deeply into Heartopia's broader gameplay loop. Your catches fuel cooking endeavors, supply crafting materials, and fulfill various quest objectives, establishing fishing as a foundational skill rather than an optional hobby.

locations.title: Fishing Locations
locations.desc: Different fish appear in different spots. Below is a quick reference; for every fish with weather and times, see the full Fish Locations Guide.

lake.title: Lakes and Ponds
lake.desc: Perfect starting zones with gentle waters. Visit Meadow Lake for Wels Catfish and Goldfish, Forest Lake for Arctic Char and Giant Pearl Mussel, or try Spring Lake where Crucian Carp become more active during rainfall.

river.title: Rivers
river.desc: Flowing waters with intermediate challenges. Giantwood River yields Huchen when you reach Level 9 during rainbow conditions, while Tranquil River's Burbot appears exclusively between noon and 6 PM.

ocean.title: Ocean
ocean.desc: Advanced saltwater zones across Zephyr, Whale (home to Atlantic Salmon and level-10 Swordfish), and Starlight Seas. Rainbow Fish appears at Starlight during rainbow weather, while Bill's boat expeditions target Golden King Crab.

hidden.title: Hidden and Special
hidden.desc: Discover Secret Pond's midnight-only Koi, plus additional locations that reveal themselves through story progression and thorough exploration.

keySpotsRows.notable: Arctic Char, Giant Pearl Mussel, Largemouth Bass

rainbowRare.title: Maximizing Rainbow Weather Opportunities
rainbowRare.desc: Rainbow conditions trigger spawns of premium species with exceptional value. Consult your smartwatch's 5-day weather outlook—rainbow symbols mark your most profitable fishing windows.
rainbowRare.topPriority: Make Bill's boat your first stop on rainbow days—Golden King Crab from sea fishing expeditions command premium prices.
rainbowRare.rareNote: Starlight Sea's Rainbow Fish (requires Premium Bait) sells for 5,000 Gold. Secret Pond's Koi only surfaces at midnight. Species like Swordfish, Bluefin Tuna, Huchen, and Arctic Char demand specific level requirements alongside favorable weather.

baitAndTools.title: Bait and Tools
baitAndTools.intro: Complete Vanya's Cat on the Roof quest to unlock bait usage. Deploy it waterside through your inventory: choose your bait, select Chum, and watch fish appear instantly.
baitAndTools.basic: Basic Bait: Craft using Vanya's apple recipe or purchase for 200 Gold. Instantly summons fish while reducing wait times between bites.
baitAndTools.premium: Premium Bait: Becomes available at Level 4. Significantly improves rare spawn rates—essential for targeting Rainbow Fish.
baitAndTools.specialized: Bill's offerings: Mermaid Perfume for rarity-specific targeting, Fish Attractor to bypass the catching minigame entirely.
baitAndTools.legendary: Legendary Bait: Earned through Vanya's daily quests after reaching Level 10. Optimized for legendary species encounters.
baitAndTools.mermaidDesc: Vanya provides this Level 4 unlock. Activate it near water while grouped with other players to trigger blue-glowing shadows containing environment-specific rarities: Edible Frog in lakes, Tilapia in rivers, Rabbit Fish in oceans. Effectiveness scales with participant count.

fishList.title: Fish List Overview
fishList.desc: Heartopia has 94 species. Location, time, and weather determine what appears. Use the Fish Observation tab and the full list for completion.

tips.title: Pro Fishing Strategies
tips.tip1: Align your fishing schedule with target species requirements—your Collections app tracks catches and hints at missing species locations.
tips.tip2: Rainbow weather demands strategic prioritization: focus on Bill's sea expeditions for Golden King Crab, then target premium spots for Huchen, Swordfish, and Goldfish.
tips.tip3: Only reel during white or yellow tension states; immediately release when red appears. Stock Repair Kits to prevent equipment failure mid-session.
tips.tip4: Maximize profits by selling premium catches (200+ Gold) uncooked while processing budget fish into dishes. Your home pond with bait creates reliable income loops.
tips.quote: Rush to Fishing Level 2 for improved star-rating odds, then religiously monitor weather forecasts for rainbow opportunities.

levelRewards.title: Progression Rewards by Level
levelRewards.desc: Advance through your smartwatch's Hobby App by accumulating Fishing Proficiency and spending Hobby Upgrade Tickets.
levelRewards.level10Note: Level 10 mastery grants: Pearl of the Tides rod (20% luck boost), Legendary Bait access, Tide Whisper ability (legendary proximity alerts), plus Vanya's daily visits offering Legendary Bait quests.

levelRows.text: Enhanced Cast technique for extended range
Premium Bait access, Mermaid Attractor tool, 4-star eligibility
Ocean Master Rod crafting (30% rare catch speed)
Pearl of the Tides equipment, Legendary Bait, Tide Whisper sense

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: What's the process for unlocking fishing?
faq.a1: Continue through the tutorial until encountering Vanya, then craft and deliver a Repair Kit. In return, you'll obtain both the Hobby Expansion Ticket and your starter fishing rod.
faq.q2: What conditions trigger rare fish spawns?
faq.a2: Most require rainbow weather conditions (visible in your smartwatch forecast), precise locations (Starlight Sea for Rainbow Fish, Secret Pond at midnight for Koi), and often Level 9 or 10 proficiency.
faq.q3: Is nighttime fishing possible?
faq.a3: Absolutely. Nocturnal fishing becomes available at Level 7, unlocking access to night-exclusive species.
faq.q4: Does the main storyline require fishing?
faq.a4: While not mandatory for story completion, fishing supplies ingredients for numerous recipes, quest objectives, and achievement criteria—making progression highly beneficial.

paintingGuide.metaTitle: Heartopia Painting Tools: Full Guide to Drawing & Design
paintingGuide.metaDesc: Master Heartopia painting tools: unlock Drawing Board, use all brushes, make custom clothes & furniture. Complete cost table, pro tips, and FAQ inside.
paintingGuide.heroDesc: The Drawing Board unlocks a full creative system — custom clothes, painted furniture, and gallery art. This guide covers every tool, both unlock quests, exact material costs, and pro tips that most guides miss.
paintingGuide.quote: Once you unlock the Drawing Board, you can paint on canvases, sew custom outfits, and decorate furniture — all from one tool. Updated February 2026 for v0.4.2.

unlock.title: How to Unlock Heartopia Painting Mode
unlock.intro: Painting unlocks through a two-quest chain at D.G. Member Level 14.
unlock.req: Reach D.G. Member Level 14 by completing daily Resident Requests.
unlock.quest1Step1: Talk to Dorothee at the Clothing Store — she gives you Jean Morin's Letter.
unlock.quest1Step2: Visit Ka Ching at the General Store and buy the Drawing Board for 2,000 Gold.
unlock.quest1Step3: Place the Drawing Board, paint something, and save it.
unlock.quest1Step4: Interact with the Drawing Board again and tap the middle icon above it to open your Gallery.
unlock.quest1Note: Hidden step: the Gallery button appears directly on the Drawing Board (middle of three icons), not in any menu. This is the most common reason players get stuck.
unlock.quest2Giver: After Brushstroke Whimsy, Ka Ching offers this quest.
unlock.quest2Step1: Use the Drawing Board to apply a clip-art design to a piece of Joinery Furniture.
unlock.quest2Step2: Apply the design at your workbench using 1 Ink.

tools.title: All Heartopia Painting Tools Explained
tools.intro: Open the Drawing Board and tap the paintbrush icon to enter the canvas editor. All tools are on the left panel.

toolRows.does: Draw and color individual pixels; adjustable size (S, M, L, XL)
toolRows.tip: Use Small for details, Extra Large for solid background fills

toolRows.does: Fills a connected area with one color instantly
toolRows.tip: Fill base layers first, then add detail on top

toolRows.does: Draws circles, squares, stars, hearts, and lines
toolRows.tip: Use shapes to build pixel art grids and borders quickly

toolRows.does: Removes pixels cleanly with adjustable size
toolRows.tip: Same sizes as the brush; use Large to clear sections fast

toolRows.does: Applies pre-made designs: flowers, animals, trees, candies, and more
toolRows.tip: Non-artists can build appealing designs using stamps alone

toolRows.does: Pre-drawn outlines you fill in — no drawing skill required
toolRows.tip: Great for beginners; structured and safe starting point

toolRows.does: Basic palette by default; tap the advanced icon for the full color picker
toolRows.tip: Build a small color swatch strip on one edge of the canvas, then eyedrop from it — faster than reopening the palette each time

toolRows.does: Overlay grid for pixel-level alignment; pinch or tap to zoom
toolRows.tip: Always enable the grid for clothing designs — it shows how patterns map onto garment seams

toolRows.does: Paints symmetrically on both sides at once
toolRows.tip: Makes symmetrical clothing patterns and logo designs much faster

toolRows.does: Reverses or restores the last brush stroke
Pre-made borders, frames, and icon sets you can stamp and resize
toolRows.tip: Use as a starting layer, then paint over or around them for custom results without starting from scratch

toolRows.does: Saves your design — top-left corner of the canvas editor
toolRows.tip: Save frequently; there is no auto-save in the paint editor

tools.pixelNote: The canvas has a limited resolution by design. Pixel art, 8-bit sprites, and retro-game character portraits look intentional and distinctive — embrace the style.

canvas.title: Canvas Types
canvas.intro: Choose your canvas based on what you are making.

rows.notes: Free to use; good starting point
Requires 1 Tailoring Ticket to finalize at workbench
Requires 1 Ink to apply at workbench

canvas.sizes: 1:1 (square), 4:3, 3:4, 16:9, 9:16 — choose based on the shape of your project.

clothing.title: How to Make Custom Clothes
clothing.step1: Open the Drawing Board → tap the paintbrush icon → select New Drawing → choose a Clothing Template.
clothing.step2: Design your outfit using any tools, then save (floppy disk icon, top-left).
clothing.step3: Go to your Workbench → tap the Scissors icon (third icon).
clothing.step4: Select the clothing type and your saved design — a preview appears on the left.
clothing.wear: Wardrobe → Custom Designs tab → equip the piece.
clothing.ticketFree: 10 free Tailoring Tickets from completing Brushstroke Whimsy.
clothing.ticketTip: Budget tip: use the 10 free tickets to experiment. Settle on a design you love before spending Moonlight Crystals.

furniture.title: How to Paint Custom Furniture
furniture.supportedNote: Only Joinery Furniture supports custom painted designs. Other furniture types can be dyed (solid color only) but not painted with custom art.
furniture.step1: Open the Drawing Board → tap the paintbrush icon → select New Drawing → choose a Furniture Template.
furniture.step3: Make sure the furniture piece is in your inventory or storage. It will not appear in the selection list if it is placed outside — this is the most common cause of confusion.
furniture.step4: Go to your Workbench → tap the Brush icon.
furniture.step5: Select the furniture item, choose your saved design, and confirm — this uses 1 Ink.
furniture.inkFree: 5 Ink from completing the Painted Furniture quest.
furniture.inkBuy: Additional Ink: 50 Moonlight Crystals each from the General Store.
furniture.dye: Go to your workbench → select any furniture → apply dye. Dye costs 100 Gold from Ka Ching, or find it free by popping blue bubbles around town.

gallery.title: Gallery and Displaying Your Art
gallery.galleryAccess: Interact with the Drawing Board → tap the middle icon of the three that appear above it. The Gallery is not inside any menu.
gallery.galleryOrganized: Your Gallery is organized into four sections: canvas paintings, clothing and shoe designs, furniture designs, and collectibles.
gallery.galleryManage: Tap the three-dot menu on any item to rename, duplicate, or delete it.
gallery.display: Place finished canvases in frames or easels inside your house. Displayed artwork is visible to friends and neighbors who visit.

rows.where: Ka Ching, General Store (unlocks at DG Lv 14)
Store / 10 free from Brushstroke Whimsy quest
General Store / 5 free from Painted Furniture quest
Ka Ching / free from blue bubbles

tips.title: Pro Tips for Better Designs
tips.tip1: Before painting, lay a row of your key colors along one edge of the canvas. Eyedrop from it instead of hunting the palette each time. Delete the strip when done.
tips.tip2: Always paint clothing with the grid visible. It reveals how the template maps onto the garment so patterns line up at seams.
tips.tip3Title: Stamps are a feature, not a shortcut
tips.tip3: Combining clip-art stamps with hand-drawn elements creates more polished designs than either approach alone. Use stamps for foliage, borders, and animals; draw unique patterns by hand.
tips.tip4Title: Decal templates as a starting layer
tips.tip4: Use the v0.4.2 decal templates as a base layer, then customize around them. Faster than starting from scratch, more personal than using them alone.
tips.tip5: Use the Gallery duplicate function to back up a design before making changes. The editor has no version history beyond undo.
tips.tip6: The canvas resolution is limited by design. Pixel art, 8-bit sprites, and retro-game portraits look intentional and are celebrated by the Heartopia community.

faq.title: Painting Tools FAQ
faq.q1: How do I unlock the painting tools?
faq.a1: Reach D.G. Member Level 14 by completing daily Resident Requests, then talk to Dorothee at the Clothing Store to start the Brushstroke Whimsy quest. Buy the Drawing Board from Ka Ching for 2,000 Gold.
faq.q2: Where is the Gallery in Heartopia?
faq.a2: The Gallery is not in any menu. Interact with the Drawing Board and tap the middle icon of the three that appear above it.
faq.q3: Why can't I find my furniture to paint it?
faq.a3: The furniture must be in your inventory or storage. If it is placed in your house or outdoors, it will not show in the selection list at the workbench.
faq.q4: What furniture can I paint in Heartopia?
faq.a4: Currently only Joinery Furniture supports custom painted designs. Other furniture types support dye (solid color) but not full custom art.
faq.q5: How many Tailoring Tickets do I get for free?
faq.a5: 10 Tailoring Tickets from completing the Brushstroke Whimsy quest. Additional tickets cost 80 Moonlight Crystals each.
faq.q6: What is the difference between Ink and Dye?
faq.a6: Ink (50 Moonlight Crystals) applies a custom painted design to furniture. Dye (100 Gold from Ka Ching, or free from blue bubbles) applies a single solid color without a design.
faq.q7: Can I share my designs with other players?
faq.a7: Yes. Paintings displayed in frames or easels in your house are visible to visiting friends and neighbors.

bugGuide.metaTitle: Heartopia Bug Catching Guide: All Insects & Locations
bugGuide.metaDesc: Catch all 76+ insects in Heartopia — locations by area, time & weather, star rating tips, trap recipes, Bait the Insects event, and gold farming routes.
bugGuide.heroDesc: The complete guide to insect catching in Heartopia — how to unlock, where every bug spawns, star rating secrets, insect traps, and the best gold-farming route. Updated February 2026.
bugGuide.quote: The rarest bugs come out after rain and at night. If you only catch bugs in sunny weather, you're missing more than half the species — and all the best gold.

unlock.title: How to Unlock Bug Catching
unlock.intro: Bug catching is one of Heartopia's seven hobbies. Here's how to get started.
unlock.req: Reach Developer Level 6 to receive a Hobby Expansion Ticket.
unlock.step1: Reach Developer Level 6 by completing Resident Requests and story tasks.
unlock.step2: Use your Hobby Expansion Ticket and select Insect Catching from the hobby menu.
unlock.step3: Find Naniwa — your insect catching mentor — near the forest or garden area.
unlock.step4: Naniwa gives you a Basic Bug Net and teaches the fundamental mechanics.
unlock.step5: Catch your first bug to officially begin your Insect Journal collection.
unlock.mentorNote: Naniwa also sells upgrades, Inflatable Insect Attractors, and repair kits. Check back as you level up.

mechanics.title: How to Catch Bugs
mechanics.intro: Insect catching is a stealth-and-timing system. Approach wrong and the bug flees.
mechanics.step1: Equip your bug net from your inventory.
mechanics.step2: Spot a bug — look for movement on trees, flowers, grass, and near water.
mechanics.step3: Walk slowly toward it. Do NOT run — bugs have a detection radius and will flee.
mechanics.step4: Stop when the net-range indicator appears near the bug.
mechanics.step5: Swing your net with a tap or click. Success depends on net level, distance, and bug rarity.
mechanics.fleeNote: If a bug flees, leave the area for a moment and return. Spawn points reset after a short cooldown.
mechanics.netIntro: Upgrade your net as you level up — higher nets have wider catch radius and better success rates against rare species.

netRows.feature: Standard range, suitable for common bugs
Larger catch area, improved rare bug success
Maximum range, best for Ultra Rare species

bugTableSection.title: All Bugs & Locations (Complete Table)

timeWeather.rainbowTip: Rainbow weather is the single best condition for rare bugs — it triggers both Sunny and Rainy spawn pools simultaneously. Check your smartwatch forecast and prioritize rainbow sessions.

starRating.title: Star Rating System Explained
starRating.intro: Catching a bug once isn't enough. Each species has a 1–5 star rating in your Insect Journal, and completing a 5-star entry requires catching larger specimens.
starRating.how1: Bug size is the primary driver of star rating — larger specimens get higher stars.
starRating.how2: There is no visible size indicator before you catch the bug. It's a random roll each catch attempt.
starRating.how3: Higher net levels improve your odds of catching higher-rarity (and larger) specimens.
starRating.how4: Catching the same species repeatedly increases your chance of eventually landing a 5-star specimen.
starRating.how5: Some species (especially Lv5-6) only yield high-star specimens once your catching level matches or exceeds their level.

stratRows.action: Catch at net level lower than bug level
Catch with Master net + high catching level

starRating.rewardNote: Completing 5-star entries for milestone numbers of species rewards Moonlight Crystals and exclusive collection titles.

tools.title: Tools, Traps & Insect Attractor
tools.trapIntro: Insect Traps passively catch bugs while you do other things. Three types exist, each craftable at your workbench.

trapRows.recipe: Nails ×3, Bug Lure ×2, Wire ×3, Wood Planks ×5

tools.attractorCost: Cost: 2,000 Gold or 1 Wishing Star from Naniwa
tools.attractorUse: Place in an area with existing bug spawns (Flower Field or Forest recommended). Attracts rare insects — currently the only reliable way to catch Sulkowsky's Morpho.
tools.attractorTip: The Attractor lasts only 1 minute and has a small area of effect. For best results: pre-catch 2-3 common bugs in the area first, then place the Attractor. The pre-catches warm up the spawn pool and improve Sulkowsky's Morpho appearance rate.
tools.attractorWarning: Do not place the Attractor in an empty area — spawn rates are much lower. Always pre-catch nearby bugs first.

baitEvent.title: Bait the Insects Event
baitEvent.intro: A periodic event that temporarily spawns exclusive bug species unavailable through normal catching.
baitEvent.eventBugs: Exclusive event bugs: Apollo, Postman Butterfly, Pink Katydid, White Witch
baitEvent.howToJoin: Check the Events tab in your in-game menu for the event schedule. The event spawns special insects at designated event zones marked on your map.
baitEvent.tip: The Bait the Insects event is the only way to catch Apollo and Postman Butterfly outside of it. Don't skip this event if you want a complete Insect Journal.

gold.title: Best Bugs for Gold — Daily Farming Route
gold.intro: Bug catching isn't the highest-earning hobby, but targeted runs yield solid passive income. Focus on rare bugs with high sell prices.
gold.route: Forest Lake Shore (Rainy/Rainbow bugs) → Flower Field: Whale Mountain (Purple Emperor + Splay-Footed Carpenter) → Onsen Mountain: Ruins (Giant Asian Mantis, Sunny) → Forest: Jump Puzzle (Stag Beetle, Rainy) → Fishing Village: Wharf (Beautiful Leopard, Night)

tips.title: Pro Tips & Troubleshooting
tips.tip1: Bugs detect sprint movement and immediately flee. Always approach by walking, especially for rare species.
tips.tip2: Some tree-trunk beetles can be shaken loose by interacting with certain trees in the Forest and Mountain areas.
tips.tip3: Rain unlocks a separate spawn pool. The bugs that appear in rain (Rainy/Rainbow) are disproportionately rare and valuable — never skip a rainy session.
tips.tip4: Jewel Beetle, Luna Moth, Atlas Moth, Green Birdwing, and Stag Beetle all spawn at night. A dedicated nighttime loop yields far more gold than daytime catching.
tips.tip5Title: Warm up spawn pools before using the Attractor
tips.tip5: Catch 2-3 common bugs near the Attractor placement spot before activating it. This significantly improves rare spawn rates within the Attractor's radius.
tips.tip6Title: Season-exclusive bugs: don't miss the window
tips.tip6: Frostspore butterflies only appear Jan 31–Mar 13. Mark your calendar. Once the season ends, you can still view them in your Journal but cannot catch new specimens until next year.

faq.title: Bug Catching FAQ
faq.q1: How do I unlock bug catching in Heartopia?
faq.a1: Reach Developer Level 6, then use a Hobby Expansion Ticket to select Insect Catching. Find Naniwa (your hobby mentor) near the forest or garden area — they'll give you a Basic Bug Net and explain the mechanics.
faq.q2: Why does the bug always run away before I can catch it?
faq.a2: You're likely running or moving too fast. Bugs have a detection radius and flee from sprint movement. Always approach by walking slowly. If a bug flees, leave the area and return after a short cooldown.
faq.q3: Where is Sulkowsky's Morpho in Heartopia?
faq.a3: Sulkowsky's Morpho can only be caught using the Inflatable Insect Attractor — it does NOT spawn normally in the wild. You need Insect Catching Level 4 to buy the Attractor from Naniwa (2,000 Gold or 1 Wishing Star). Pre-catch 2-3 nearby bugs before placing the Attractor for best results.
faq.q4: How do I get 5-star bugs in my Insect Journal?
faq.a4: Star rating is determined by specimen size, which is a random factor each catch. Catching with a higher-level net and at a catching level that matches or exceeds the bug's level improves your odds. Catch the same species repeatedly until you land a large specimen.
faq.q5: What bugs only appear during Rainy or Rainbow weather?
faq.a5: Rainy/Rainbow-exclusive bugs include: Amethyst Flower Beetle, Four-Spotted Skimmer, Seven-Spotted Ladybug, Four-Spotted Ladybug, Common Whitetail, Beautiful Leopard, Stag Beetle, Beautiful Demoiselle, and Asian Lady Beetle. Rainbow weather also unlocks the Sunny pool simultaneously — making it the best single condition for collection progress.
faq.q6: What is the Bait the Insects event and when does it happen?
faq.a6: Bait the Insects is a periodic in-game event that spawns exclusive bugs unavailable through normal catching — including Apollo, Postman Butterfly, Pink Katydid, and White Witch. Check the Events tab in your menu for the schedule. Missing it means missing those species until the next occurrence.
faq.q7: Are Frostspore butterflies part of the regular bug catching hobby?
faq.a7: Yes — the five Frostspore butterflies (Mother-of-Pearl, Purple Spotted Swallowtail, Queen Alexandra's Birdwing, Sulkowsky's Morpho, and Postman's Butterfly) count toward your Insect Journal. They only appear during Winter Frost Season (Jan 31–Mar 13). See our dedicated Frostspore Butterflies guide for exact spawn locations.

fishLocations.metaTitle: Fish Locations in Heartopia – Where to Find Every Fish
fishLocations.metaDesc: Discover all fish locations in Heartopia! Our complete guide covers lakes, rivers, and coastal waters, including exact spots to find rare and legendary species.
fishLocations.heroDesc: Knowing where to fish is essential if you want to collect specific fish in Heartopia. Different bodies of water support different species, and many fish can only be found in certain locations or under specific conditions.
fishLocations.intro: This guide provides a complete overview of all fish locations in Heartopia, explaining where to find fish, how locations differ, and where rare fish are most likely to appear.
fishLocations.mechanicsLink: For a full overview of fishing mechanics, see the Fishing Guide in Heartopia.
fishLocations.overviewDesc: Heartopia divides fishing areas into several natural categories. Each category has its own environment, fish pool, and progression role. The main fishing locations include: Lakes and ponds, Rivers and streams, Coastal waters, and Special and hidden fishing spots. Understanding these locations will help you find the fish you need more efficiently.

lake.title: Lakes and Ponds
lake.desc1: Lakes and ponds are usually the first fishing locations players encounter in Heartopia. These calm bodies of water are easy to access and ideal for beginners.
lake.subTitle: What You Can Find in Lakes
lake.text: Lakes mainly contain common freshwater fish. These fish are often used for early cooking recipes, crafting, and introductory quests. Typical lake fish include Common Carp, Pond Minnow, Silver Carp, and Night Catfish (appears at night). Lakes are also a good place to practice fishing without pressure, as fish here usually have simple requirements.

river.title: Rivers and Streams
river.desc1: Rivers and streams add more variety to fishing in Heartopia. Because rivers flow through multiple regions, they often contain a wider range of fish.
river.subTitle: River Fishing Characteristics
river.li2: Some fish only appear in rivers
river.li3: Certain river sections unlock later in the game
river.text: Common river fish include River Bass, Silver Minnow, Bluefin Bass, and Moonlight Eel (night only). If you are searching for mid-game fish or recipe ingredients, rivers are often the best choice.

ocean.title: Coastal Waters
ocean.desc1: Coastal waters represent a major step forward in fishing progression. These locations are usually unlocked later and contain saltwater fish and higher-value species.
ocean.subTitle: Why Coastal Fishing Matters
ocean.li1: Access to rare and valuable fish
ocean.li3: Required for certain quests and achievements
ocean.text: Typical coastal fish include Coastal Snapper, Sea Bream, Tide Runner Fish, and Deepwater Mackerel. Coastal fishing often rewards patience and exploration, as some areas may not be immediately visible on the map.

hidden.title: Special and Hidden Fishing Spots
hidden.desc1: Not all fish locations in Heartopia are clearly marked. Some fishing spots are hidden or unlocked only through exploration or story progression.
hidden.subTitle: How to Find Hidden Fishing Spots
hidden.text: Hidden fishing spots often contain rare fish, location-exclusive species, and special quest-related fish. If you are searching for rare fish, these locations are worth the effort.

rare.title: Rare Fish Locations in Heartopia
rare.desc1: Rare fish usually appear under stricter conditions. They may require a specific location, a certain time of day, or game progression milestones.
rare.subTitle: Where Rare Fish Are Most Common
rare.useTitle: What Rare Fish Are Used For
rare.text: Rare fish are often used for special cooking recipes, high-level crafting, and quest objectives. For cooking-related uses, you can check recipes like Mushroom Pie.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Where can I find fish in Heartopia?
faq.a1: Fish can be found in lakes, rivers, coastal waters, and special hidden locations throughout Heartopia.
faq.q2: Are fish location-specific?
faq.a2: Yes. Many fish only appear in certain locations, and some require specific conditions such as time of day.
faq.q3: Do rare fish have fixed locations?
faq.a3: Most rare fish appear in limited locations, often in coastal or hidden fishing spots.
faq.q4: Is fishing location more important than time?
faq.a4: Both matter. Location determines which fish can appear, while time of day affects availability.

dorisLocation.metaTitle: Where Is Doris in Heartopia? Location by Weather & Time
dorisLocation.metaDesc: Find Doris's exact location in Heartopia based on weather and time, including rain, snowfall, rainbow, and meteor shower conditions.
dorisLocation.heroDesc: Doris does not stay in one fixed place. In Heartopia, Doris appears in different locations depending on the current weather event.
dorisLocation.intro: If you cannot find Doris, the reason is almost always weather-related, not the map itself.

quickAnswer.title: Quick Answer: Doris Location by Weather
quickAnswer.subtitle: Doris appears only during special weather events. Her location changes based on the active weather at that moment.
quickAnswer.rainbowLocation: At the base of the rainbow
quickAnswer.noWeather: If none of these weather conditions are active, Doris will not appear anywhere.

rain.title: Rain or Active Snowfall
rain.desc: When it is raining or actively snowing, Doris can be found on Art Street, usually standing in an open outdoor area rather than inside buildings. This is the most common situation where players encounter Doris.

rainbow.title: Rainbow Weather
rainbow.location: Location: At the base of the rainbow
rainbow.desc: During rainbow weather, Doris appears near the visible rainbow itself, not at her usual rain location. You must physically travel to where the rainbow touches the ground. She will not appear elsewhere during this event.

meteor.title: Meteor Shower
meteor.desc: During a meteor shower, Doris relocates to the Onsen Mountain region. This location is easy to miss if you are checking only low-level areas. Meteor showers are limited-time events, so Doris will disappear once the event ends.

snowWarning.title: Important: Snowy Ground Is NOT Snowfall
snowWarning.subtitle: This is one of the most common reasons players cannot find Doris.
snowWarning.p1: A snowy environment does not mean snowfall is active.
snowWarning.snowGround: Snow on the ground = ❌ not enough
snowWarning.snowFalling: Snow visibly falling from the sky = ✅ required
snowWarning.footer: Doris only appears during active snowfall, not static winter scenery.

checklist.title: Why Doris Is Not Showing Up (Checklist)
checklist.intro: If you still cannot find Doris, check the following in order:
checklist.item1: Is there an active weather event (rain, snowfall, rainbow, or meteor shower)?
checklist.item2: Is the snow actually falling (not just covering the ground)?
checklist.item3: Are you in the correct location for the current weather?
checklist.item4: Has the weather event already ended?
checklist.item5: Did you reload the area after the weather changed?
checklist.footer: In most cases, checking the weather again solves the issue.

whatDoes.title: What Does Doris Do in Heartopia?
whatDoes.intro: Doris is a weather-dependent NPC associated with limited-time items and recipes. Depending on the weather, Doris may offer:
whatDoes.item1: Special recipes (such as Roll Cake–related items)
whatDoes.item3: Unique furniture or emotes during meteor showers
whatDoes.footer: Because her appearance is tied to weather, she is easy to miss without knowing the conditions.

howToCheck.title: How to Check Weather in Heartopia
howToCheck.intro: To find Doris efficiently, always check the weather first:
howToCheck.step1: Look for visible weather effects (rain, falling snow, rainbow, meteors)
howToCheck.step2: Wait for weather changes if none are active
howToCheck.step3: Revisit the area after the weather updates
howToCheck.footer: Planning around weather saves a lot of time.

rain.title: Rain & Snowfall Shop Items
rain.subtitle: When it is raining or snowfalling, head over to the Art Street district of the main town. She will be there with her stall, ready to trade regardless of the downpour.
rain.note: One thing you need to note is that a snowy day and snowfall are very different, so do not get confused.
rain.intro: Doris' shop is the exclusive source for several essential items. If you're planning to complete your collection, make sure you have enough currency saved up. During the rains, you can get the following from her shop:
items: The Roll Cake Recipe
Coloured Sugar, the essential item for crafting the different varieties of Roll Cakes
Umbrellas
Postcards
rain.imageAlt: Doris location during rain in Heartopia on Art Street

rainbow.title: Rainbow Shop Items
rainbow.subtitle: Just when you think you've missed Doris in the rain, she has one more trick up her sleeve. After the rain clears, keep your eyes on the sky! If a Rainbow appears, Doris will relocate to be near its base.
rainbow.location: Unlike her fixed spot on Art Street during the rain, Doris follows the rainbow, which can appear in various scenic locations across Heartopia. Common sightings include: the Onsen mountain and the forest.
rainbow.note: Because the rainbow can appear in different spots, you may need to do a little bit of exploring to find the exact location of the colorful arc!
rainbow.intro: When Doris appears under a rainbow, her shop features a mix of baking essentials and a very special collectible:
items: The Roll Cake Recipe
Coloured Sugar
Rainbow Emote
Postcards
rainbow.imageAlt: Doris location during rainbow weather in Heartopia

meteor.title: Meteor Shower Shop Items
meteor.subtitle: When a Meteor Shower occurs, you can find Doris in Heartopia. You can find her where the meteors land (mostly on Onsen mountain), typically in open clearings or near the beach, where she sets up her temporary shop.
meteor.special: Beyond the Roll Cake recipes, Doris is the only source for Meteorite Furniture and other celestial-themed decor that cannot be found anywhere else in Heartopia.
meteor.currency: At this location, instead of coins, Doris only accepts Star Fragments as currency and sells:
items: Meteorite Furniture
Celestial-themed decor
Special meteor shower exclusive items
meteor.imageAlt: Doris location during meteor shower in Heartopia on Onsen Mountain

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Why can't I find Doris even though it's snowing?
faq.a1: Because the snow may be static. Doris only appears during active snowfall.
faq.q2: Does Doris appear every day?
faq.a2: No. Doris only appears during specific weather events.
faq.q3: Does Doris move randomly?
faq.a3: No. Her location is fixed for each weather type.
faq.q4: Can Doris disappear suddenly?
faq.a4: Yes. Once the weather event ends, Doris disappears.

mushroomPie.metaTitle: Mushroom Pie Recipe in Heartopia – Ingredients & Steps
mushroomPie.metaDesc: Master the Mushroom Pie recipe in Heartopia! Learn where to find mushrooms and dough, followed by follow our easy step-by-step cooking guide and design tips.
mushroomPie.heroDesc: Mushroom Pie is a classic cooking dish in Heartopia, valued for both its simplicity and usefulness. It is commonly used for cooking progression, quest requirements, and general gameplay support.
mushroomPie.intro: This guide explains how to make Mushroom Pie in Heartopia, including the required ingredients, where to find them, and how the dish is typically used in the game. If you are new to cooking or gathering resources, this recipe is an excellent place to start.

whatIs.title: What Is Mushroom Pie in Heartopia?
whatIs.p1: Mushroom Pie is a cooked food item made using basic ingredients found early to mid-game. Unlike advanced dishes, it does not require rare materials or complex preparation, which makes it accessible to most players.
whatIs.p2: In Heartopia, Mushroom Pie represents comfort food rather than a high-tier item. Its value lies in: Ease of preparation, Commonly available ingredients, and Frequent use in quests or NPC requests. Because of this, Mushroom Pie is often one of the first structured recipes players learn.

ingredients.title: Mushroom Pie Ingredients
ingredients.p1: To make Mushroom Pie in Heartopia, you need the following required ingredients: Mushroom and Dough. Both ingredients can be obtained through normal gameplay without special tools.
ingredients.sourceText: Mushrooms are commonly found in natural environments across Heartopia. Typical sources include forested areas, grasslands near trees, and areas with high vegetation density. Mushrooms may respawn over time, making them a reliable ingredient for repeated cooking.
ingredients.doughText: Dough is usually obtained through basic crafting, cooking-related systems, or purchasing from certain vendors (if available in your progression stage). Because Dough is a base cooking ingredient, it is often unlocked early and reused across multiple recipes.

steps.title: How to Make Mushroom Pie in Heartopia
steps.p1: Once you have collected the ingredients, follow these steps to cook Mushroom Pie:
steps.step2: Select Mushroom Pie from the recipe list
steps.step3: Add the required ingredients: Mushroom × 1, Dough × 1
steps.p2: After cooking, Mushroom Pie will be added to your inventory and can be used immediately or saved for later.

uses.title: What Is Mushroom Pie Used For?
uses.item1Desc: It can be consumed as part of the cooking system, contributing to general gameplay balance.
uses.item2Desc: Some quests or character interactions may require Mushroom Pie as a delivery item.
uses.item3Desc: Because it is easy to craft, Mushroom Pie is often used to practice cooking mechanics or fulfill early progression requirements.
uses.guideLink: For other dishes and cooking-related content, see the Heartopia Cooking & Recipes Guide.

tips.title: Tips for Making Mushroom Pie Efficiently
tips.tip1: Collect mushrooms while exploring instead of farming them separately
tips.tip2: Keep a small supply of Dough ready if you plan to cook often
tips.tip3: Use Mushroom Pie early rather than saving it unnecessarily
tips.footer: Cooking simple recipes regularly helps you stay familiar with the system and avoids inventory overload.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do you unlock Mushroom Pie in Heartopia?
faq.a1: Mushroom Pie becomes available through normal cooking progression. No special quest is required.
faq.q2: Where can I find mushrooms easily?
faq.a2: Mushrooms are commonly found in forested or grassy areas and respawn over time.
faq.q3: Is Mushroom Pie required for quests?
faq.a3: Some quests or NPC requests may ask for Mushroom Pie, especially early in the game.
faq.q4: Can Mushroom Pie be bought instead of cooked?
faq.a4: Availability depends on progression and vendors. Cooking is the most reliable method.

online.text: Heartopia includes both offline and online elements. Core gameplay can be enjoyed offline, but some modes require a connection.
online.desc1: Some game modes or features may require an internet connection, updates, or interactions.
online.desc2: Core gameplay can typically be enjoyed offline; progression and exploration do not always require a connection.

housing.metaTitle: Heartopia House Ideas & Blueprints – Layouts and Design Tips
housing.metaDesc: Explore creative Heartopia house ideas and expert blueprint concepts, including room layouts, functional planning, and tips to design your dream aesthetic home.
housing.intro: Housing in Heartopia is flexible and player-driven, emphasizing functional flow over rigid templates. This guide combines blueprint planning concepts with practical upgrades, furniture, and design inspiration to help you build efficiently and beautifully.
housing.detailIntro: This guide focuses on Heartopia house ideas and blueprint-style planning, helping you understand how to design a house, not just how it looks.

howItWorks.title: How Housing Works in Heartopia
howItWorks.text: Housing in Heartopia is designed to feel natural and immersive. Rather than forcing fixed building templates, the game allows you to arrange rooms and spaces based on your priorities. Blueprint planning avoids wasted space and constant rebuilding.
howItWorks.li2: Emphasis on personal style and functional flow
howItWorks.li3: Easy to redesign and improve over time
howItWorks.footer: Because of this freedom, planning a house using blueprint concepts helps avoid wasted space and constant rebuilding.

upgrades.title: House Upgrades & Expansion Path
upgrades.text: Unlock plots via D.G. Level (Daily Resident Requests + Bob the Architect). Higher D.G. Rank unlocks larger building areas and new specialized features.

rows.features: Trial Mode (free building simulation), basic single-layer
Blueprint System (Save/Load designs), Co-Build with friends

upgrades.tip: Tip: Complete Daily Resident Requests to raise your D.G. Rank efficiently. Talk to Bob the Architect to check specific expansion costs.

blueprintsSystem.title: Blueprint System Explained
blueprintsSystem.text: Blueprints (unlocked at D.G. Lv8 via the Builder Tablet App) are saved design 'loadouts' — not restricted templates. They allow you to experiment with different styles without risk.

steps.title: Enter Build Mode
steps.desc: Use the hammer icon or your phone's Builder Tablet. Tip: Switch to clear weather for better rooftop placement.

steps.title: Save Blueprint
steps.desc: Open the Blueprint App and select 'Save'. You have multiple slots (e.g., Slot 1: Cottage, Slot 2: Mansion).

steps.title: Import / Load
steps.desc: Instantly switch your plot layout. Use Co-Build (Lv8+) to invite friends to help build.

steps.title: Get New Blueprints
steps.desc: Obtain from NPCs (Massimo, Ka Ching), Prize Bubbles, or the Research Room.

blueprintsSystem.advanced: Advanced Tip: Drag and copy walls/floors with pre-applied dyes to save time.

popularIdeas.title: Popular House Design Ideas
popularIdeas.text: Players usually approach housing with different goals in mind. Below are some of the most common and practical house ideas based on community trends.

style1.title: Cozy Cottage Style
style1.desc: The cozy cottage focuses on warmth, simplicity, and comfort with compact zones.
style1.bestFor: New players, relaxed gameplay, small spaces
style1.traits: Symmetrical paths, warm lighting, Sea Salt themes

style2.title: Farm-Style House
style2.desc: Practical layouts that prioritize storage and support daily resource gathering.
style2.traits: Clear functional zones, easy storage access, efficient for farming

style3.title: Modern Minimal Layout
style3.desc: Clean lines and efficient space usage with multipurpose rooms.
style3.traits: Symmetrical layouts, open floor plans, clear movement paths

style4.title: Zoo / Aquarium Theme
style4.desc: Showcasing collections with specialized animal furniture and enclosures.
style4.traits: Fenced enclosures, aquarium furniture, pet interaction spots

furniture.title: Furniture Sets & Bonuses
furniture.text: Furniture is categorized into Living, Bedroom, Kitchen, Outdoor, and Decor. Certain sets provide stat bonuses or unique interactions.
furniture.dyeTip: Dyeing: Use the Furniture tab and 'Dye' tool (from shop/events) to customize colors.

howToCreate.title: How to Create Your Own Blueprint
howToCreate.text: Effective planning follows functional flow. Ask yourself: What activities happen here? How often do I move between rooms?
howToCreate.li1: List the rooms you use most frequently
howToCreate.li2: Cluster high-use areas (e.g., kitchen near storage)
howToCreate.li3: Separate quiet zones (Bedroom) from active zones
howToCreate.li4: Reserve space for future expansion plots
howToCreate.footer: Avoid common mistakes: Ignore flow, fill all space too early, or prioritizing looks over movement.

gameplayImpact.title: Gameplay Impact of Good Design
gameplayImpact.text: While layouts don't change base stats, they provide huge convenience for daily routines.
gameplayImpact.li3: Improved flow for long decoration sessions
gameplayImpact.footer: No direct stats, but huge convenience: Faster routines, less backtracking, better long sessions.

faq.title: Housing FAQ & Pro Tips
faq.q1: Are blueprints fixed templates?
faq.a1: No — they are logical plans that are reusable across slots and can be modified at any time.
faq.q2: Can I copy builds from other players?
faq.a2: You can use Co-Build to work Together, or share design codes (future feature). Always backup your blueprint before inviting others!
faq.q3: Is there a gold limit for building?
faq.a3: Build Mode has a budget. Use external 'back-up panels' if you hit the local plot limit.
faq.q4: Where do I get Rare Timber for expansion?
faq.a4: Roaming Oak Timber drops from specific forest events and high-level gathering spots.
faq.q5: How do I save a design?
faq.a5: Use the 'Save' button in your Blueprint App (Builder Tablet) at any time to freeze your current layout.

blueprints.metaTitle: Heartopia Housing Blueprints – Layout Planning Guide
blueprints.metaDesc: Learn how to design housing blueprints in Heartopia, including room layouts, functional planning, and blueprint concepts for different playstyles.
blueprints.intro: In Heartopia, a blueprint is not a fixed building template. Instead, it is a planning approach that helps you decide where rooms go, how spaces connect, and how your house supports daily gameplay.
blueprints.detailIntro: This guide explains housing blueprints in Heartopia using clear concepts and reusable layouts, so you can design houses that remain useful as your game progresses.

whatIs.title: What Is a Housing Blueprint in Heartopia?
whatIs.text: A housing blueprint is a logical layout plan, not a single image or preset design. A good blueprint defines room placement, movement flow, and space for expansion.

whyMatters.title: Why Blueprint Planning Matters
whyMatters.text1: Without a blueprint, houses often grow randomly. This leads to long walking distances, poor room connections, and frequent redesigns.
whyMatters.text2: With blueprint planning, you gain efficient daily routines, clear functional zones, and better long-term flexibility.

p1.title: Functional Proximity
p1.desc: Rooms you use together should be close together (e.g., cooking near storage).

p2.title: Clear Movement Flow
p2.desc: Avoid layouts that force unnecessary backtracking. Use straight or circular paths.

p3.title: Expansion Awareness
p3.desc: Leave open space for future rooms or upgrades to avoid full redesigns.

starter.title: Starter Blueprint
starter.desc: Focuses on simplicity and efficiency for early gameplay.
starter.features: Short walking distance, easy to modify.

functional.title: Functional Blueprint
functional.desc: Optimized for players who focus on routine efficiency.
functional.features: Fast access to frequently used rooms.

aesthetic.title: Aesthetic Blueprint
aesthetic.desc: Designed around visual balance rather than just efficiency.

hybrid.title: Hybrid Blueprint
hybrid.desc: Combines function and aesthetics for balanced gameplay.
hybrid.features: Functional core with decorative outer sections.

steps.title: How to Create Your Own Blueprint
steps.li1: List the rooms you use most often

performance.title: Gameplay Impact
performance.text: Blueprints influence time efficiency, comfort during long play sessions, and ease of daily routines.

faq.title: Blueprints FAQ
faq.q1: Are housing blueprints fixed in Heartopia?
faq.a1: No. Blueprints are flexible concepts and can be adapted freely.
faq.q2: Can I copy blueprints from other players?
faq.a2: You can reuse ideas, but layouts should be adjusted to your available space.
faq.q3: Should I plan everything before building?
faq.a3: Basic planning is enough. Blueprints can evolve over time.

faq.a4: Not required, but highly recommended for long-term builds.

piano.metaTitle: Heartopia Piano: Get Sheet Music, Songs & Pro Playing Tips
piano.metaDesc: Master the Heartopia piano with our ultimate guide. Find free sheet music codes, unlock rare instruments, and learn how to play popular songs in-game today!
piano.heroTitle: Heartopia Piano: The Ultimate Guide to Mastery, Music Sheets, and Decor
piano.heroDesc: Whether you are looking to serenade your neighbors or host a grand concert in your virtual villa, the Heartopia piano is one of the most expressive and rewarding features in the game.
piano.intro: As the community grows in 2026, music has become the universal language of this cozy simulation world. In this comprehensive guide, we will dive deep into everything related to the Heartopia piano, from unlocking your first instrument to mastering complex Heartopia piano sheet music.

howToGet.title: How to Get Your First Heartopia Piano
howToGet.desc: Before you can start composing hits, you need to know how to acquire this centerpiece. There are several ways to obtain a Heartopia piano, depending on your progression level and budget.

store.title: Purchasing from the Furniture Store
store.desc: The most direct route is visiting the city's furniture hub. The basic upright Heartopia piano is often available for a reasonable amount of in-game currency. However, keep an eye on the rotating stock; premium grand pianos in gold or ivory finishes occasionally appear as limited-time luxury items.

leveling.title: Leveling Up Your Musical Hobby
leveling.desc: Heartopia rewards dedication. By participating in music-related daily tasks, you can increase your "Musicality" level. High-tier Heartopia piano models, which feature better sound resonance and unique visual effects, are often locked behind these hobby milestones.

mechanics.title: Mastering the Heartopia Piano Mechanics
mechanics.desc: Playing the Heartopia piano is more than just clicking buttons; it is a rhythmic experience that allows for genuine creativity.

modes.title: Basic vs. Free Play Mode
modes.desc: The game offers two distinct ways to interact with the instrument. In the basic mode, players follow on-screen prompts to play pre-loaded tracks. However, the real magic happens in Free Play Mode. Here, the Heartopia piano keyboard corresponds to specific keys on your device, allowing you to play any melody you can imagine.

mapping.title: Understanding the Key Mapping
mapping.desc: For PC users, the Heartopia piano typically maps the middle octave to the home row of your keyboard. Learning this layout is essential if you want to use Heartopia piano codes found in community forums. Practice moving your fingers between the rows to handle sharps and flats smoothly.

sheetMusic.title: Heartopia Piano Sheet Music and Song Codes
sheetMusic.desc: The most sought-after resource in the community is Heartopia piano sheet music. Since the game doesn't use traditional staves, players use a "Letter Code" system.

popular.title: Popular Heartopia Piano Song Codes
popular.desc: To help you get started, here are a few simplified sequences for the Heartopia piano:
popular.twinkle: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star: C C G G A A G - F F E E D D C
popular.lullaby: Lullaby Themes: Often utilize the softer tones of the Heartopia piano to create a relaxing atmosphere in your home.
popular.footer: We update our Heartopia piano sheet music database weekly. Many players are now sharing "script-friendly" formats, though we recommend learning to play manually to fully enjoy the "slow life" essence of the game.

simulator.tip: Pro Tip: Follow our numbered sheet music below!
simulator.instruction: Click the buttons to hear the piano notes. The layout matches the in-game Heartopia piano interface.

designing.title: Designing the Perfect Piano Room
designing.desc: A Heartopia piano shouldn't just sit in a corner. It should be the heart of your home.

acoustic.title: Acoustic Room Layouts
acoustic.desc: Use rugs and wooden flooring to complement the aesthetic of your Heartopia piano. Placing large windows behind a grand piano creates a stunning silhouette during the in-game "Golden Hour," making it the perfect spot for taking screenshots to share on social media.

group.title: Group Jam Sessions
group.desc: Did you know you can sync your Heartopia piano play with friends? If two players have pianos in the same vicinity, the game's audio engine attempts to harmonize the output. This has led to the rise of "Heartopia Orchestras," where players coordinate Heartopia piano performances for the entire server.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting Common Heartopia Piano Issues
troubleshooting.desc: Sometimes, your musical journey might hit a snag. Here is how to fix common Heartopia piano problems:
troubleshooting.latency: Audio Latency: If you notice a delay when pressing keys on your Heartopia piano, try lowering your graphics settings or checking your ping.
troubleshooting.missing: Missing Notes: Ensure your "Musicality" level is high enough to access the full 88-key range, as some beginner instruments have a limited scale.

why.title: Why the Heartopia Piano is a Must-Have
why.desc: The Heartopia piano represents the soul of the game's creative community. It bridges the gap between a simple furniture item and a powerful tool for self-expression. By mastering the Heartopia piano, you aren't just playing a game; you are contributing to the vibrant culture of the world.
why.cta: Start practicing today, collect your favorite Heartopia piano sheet music, and turn your virtual home into a masterpiece of sound and style.

faq.title: Heartopia Piano FAQ
faq.q1: How do I unlock the piano hobby in Heartopia?
faq.a1: To unlock the piano hobby, you must first reach Citizen Level 5. Once reached, visit the City Hall or the Hobby Mentor near the fountain. Complete the "Musical Soul" introductory quest to receive your first beginner keyboard.
faq.q2: Why can't I hear my Heartopia piano sound?
faq.a2: First, check your Settings > Audio to ensure "Instrument Volume" is turned up. If the issue persists, ensure you aren't in a "Silent Zone" or that your character's "Energy Meter" isn't depleted, as some advanced performances require energy.
faq.q3: Are there piano scripts or auto-players for Heartopia?
faq.a3: While third-party auto-clickers exist, using them can lead to a permanent account ban for violating Heartopia's Fair Play policy. We recommend using our Heartopia piano sheet music to learn and play manually for the best experience.
faq.q4: Can I play the Heartopia piano on mobile?
faq.a4: Yes! On mobile, the piano interface switches to a touch-sensitive layout. You can toggle between "Simple Mode" (numbered buttons) and "Pro Mode" (full keyboard) in the instrument's interface settings.
faq.q5: How many types of pianos are there in the game?
faq.a5: As of the 2026 update, there are four main types: The Upright Practice Piano, the Grand Concert Piano, the Digital Synthesizer, and the rare "Moonlight" Limited Edition Grand. Each has unique sound samples and reverb effects.

meteorShower.heroDesc: The night sky over Onsen Mountain lights up during Heartopia's Meteor Shower (Shooting Stars event) in Winter Frost Season. This rare weather event drops Starfall Shards from 9 fixed mining nodes for trading with Doris at her temporary Fortune Store.
meteorShower.intro: Collect shards for exclusive meteor furniture, Tokens, and achievements. Trigger: random night after 8PM server time (UTC-5); check Watch/Phone for the shooting star icon. Event lasts ~6 hours; shards available until 6AM reset.
meteorShower.imageAltDoris: Doris at Onsen Mountain during Meteor Shower

understanding.title: Understanding the Heartopia Meteor Shower Mechanics
understanding.desc: Before you head into the mountains, you need to know when and where the event occurs.

predict.title: How to Predict a Meteor Shower
predict.text: Check your in-game Watch or Phone forecast for the shooting star icon. The event triggers on random nights after 8PM server time (UTC-5) and lasts ~6 hours; Starfall Ore nodes are mineable until the 6AM daily reset. Peak dates often include Feb 7 and Feb 14.

legend.title: Shard Colors & Vendor
legend.text: Shards come in White, Yellow, Purple, and Blue (colors vary per event). Trade white shards for other colors at Doris. During the shower, Doris relocates to the Onsen Mountain bus stop or festival hub campfire—she only appears while the event is active.

serverTime.title: Server Time Converter (Meteor Shower)
serverTime.desc: Shards spawn in fixed hotspots every event. Use this table to plan when meteors are visible and when mining is possible in your timezone (example: Feb 14 event).

locations.title: All 9 Starfall Shard Locations Map
locations.desc: During every Heartopia meteor shower, exactly nine Starfall Ore nodes spawn in fixed hotspots (not random). Use a pickaxe to mine them. Primary route: 15–20 minutes to collect all 9. High ground is best for viewing and mining.

north.title: North Hills Slopes & Onsen Peak (3 shards)
north.item1: North Hills Slopes (2 shards): Grassy northern slopes; light climbing to reach the glowing ores.
north.item2: Onsen Peak (1 shard): Highest rock formations on the mountain.

lakeside.title: Central Lake & Pine Forest (5 shards)
lakeside.item1: Central Lake Edges (3 shards): Small docks and flat rocks by the water.
lakeside.item2: Pine Forest Shadows (2 shards): Under tree canopies; glow visible on trunks at night.

road.title: Onsen Bus Stop Path (1 shard)
road.text: One shard is on the main path near the bus stop—easy to grab on your way to find Doris.

doris.title: Finding Doris and the Fortune Store
doris.desc: Doris is the key to spending your hard-earned shards. During a Heartopia meteor shower, she leaves her usual Art Street spot and relocates to Onsen Mountain.

location.title: Doris's Location at Onsen Mountain
location.text: You can find Doris waiting near the Onsen Mountain bus stop or the festival hub, usually standing near a campfire under the falling stars. She will only trade with you while the event is active, so don't wait until the sun comes up!

rewards.title: Exclusive Rewards and Achievement
rewards.text: Doris offers a "Meteorite" series of furniture, including a stunning meteor shower room, chandeliers, and sinks.
rewards.priority: Top Priority: Purchase the "Wish Upon Shooting Stars" emote for 6 shards.
rewards.achievement: Rare Achievement: Use this emote with a friend precisely when a huge meteor passes by to unlock the "Beneath the Meteor Shower" badge for your player card.

priceList.title: Complete Meteorite Series Price List
priceList.desc: During the Heartopia meteor shower, players mine 9 meteorite ores on Onsen Mountain to obtain Starfall Shards, which serve as currency to exchange for exclusive items at Doris's Fortune Store.

regular.title: Doris Regular Items (Wishing Stars / Gold)
regular.desc: Besides meteor-exclusive furniture, Doris also sells:
regular.item1: Original Roll Cake Recipe: 50 Wishing Stars (+35 stamina).

tips.title: Pro Tips for Starfall Success
tips.desc: To maximize your efficiency during the Heartopia meteor shower, keep these tips in mind:

tip1.title: Inventory Space
tip1.text: Shards come in multiple colors and don't always stack perfectly. Clear your bag before the event starts.

tip2.title: Speed is Key
tip2.text: Use a bicycle or transport to move between the 9 locations quickly before the 6:00 AM server reset.

tip3.title: Collect 60 Shards
tip3.text: Harvesting 60 total shards across multiple events will grant you the "Stardust Collector" achievement and the "Star Picker" title.

strategy.title: Shopping & Saving Strategy
strategy.desc: Make the most of your Starfall Shards with these smart strategies:

conversion.title: Shard Conversion
conversion.text: If you don't have enough of a specific color shard, you can use white ore for proportional conversion.

stockpile.title: Stockpile Mechanism
stockpile.text: Starfall Shards do not disappear after the event ends and can be accumulated across sessions. If you can't afford the 20-shard Shower Room this time, save up and wait for the next Heartopia meteor shower.

achievement.title: Achievement Completion
achievement.text: One meteor shower yields approximately 35 shards. To earn the "Star Picker" title, you need to collect 60 total shards, which typically requires participating in two Heartopia meteor shower events.

faq.title: Heartopia Meteor Shower FAQ
faq.q1: When does the meteor shower event happen in Heartopia?
faq.a1: Random nights; check your in-game Watch or Phone for the shooting star icon. The event starts after 8PM server time (UTC-5) and lasts ~6 hours; shards can be mined until the 6AM daily reset. Peak dates often include Feb 7 and Feb 14.
faq.q2: How many starfall shards can I collect in one event?
faq.a2: Exactly 9 Starfall Ore nodes spawn each event. You can collect about 35–50 shards per meteor shower depending on mining yield.
faq.q3: Where is Doris located during the meteor shower?
faq.a3: Doris leaves her usual Art Street spot and moves to Onsen Mountain during a Heartopia meteor shower. You can find her standing near a campfire by the Onsen Mountain bus stop or the main festival hub in the mountains.
faq.q4: What is the "Beneath the Meteor Shower" hidden achievement?
faq.a4: This is a rare badge earned by using the "Wish Upon Shooting Stars" emote with a friend precisely when a large, rare meteor passes by. You will hear a unique "meteor warning" sound effect right before the huge meteor appears. You must be level 15 or higher to unlock this achievement.
faq.q5: Can I save starfall shards for the next meteor shower?
faq.a5: Yes! Starfall shards are permanent items and do not disappear from your inventory after the event ends. You can stockpile shards from multiple Heartopia meteor shower events to save up for expensive items like the Meteorite Shower Room (20 shards).
faq.q6: How do I get the "Star Picker" title?
faq.a6: You need to complete the "Stardust Collector" achievement, which requires collecting a total of 60 starfall shards. Since one event only provides about 35 shards, it will take at least two Heartopia meteor shower events to earn this title.

onsenEgg.metaTitle: Onsen Egg Heartopia: Complete the Promise Quest
onsenEgg.metaDesc: Find the Onsen Egg Heartopia in Winter Frost Season! Our guide reveals exact Day 1 egg location, step-by-step quest walkthrough, and rewards.
onsenEgg.heroTitle: Heartopia Egg Hunting Guide: Find Every Onsen Egg & Complete the Promise Quest
onsenEgg.heroDesc: Searching for the Onsen Egg Heartopia? You're in the right place! The Onsen Egg Promise is the exciting Day 1 challenge in Heartopia's Winter Frost Season egg hunt. This Onsen Egg Heartopia quest, part of the Naughty Quest series, tasks you with finding a hidden gift egg on Onsen Mountain and snapping a photo to claim rewards.
onsenEgg.intro: Our complete Onsen Egg Promise Heartopia guide covers everything: exact Onsen Egg Heartopia location, step-by-step instructions, rewards, tips, and troubleshooting. Don't miss out on Festival Tokens and exclusive recipes!

what.title: What is the Onsen Egg Promise in Heartopia?
what.desc: The Onsen Egg Promise Heartopia kicks off the 16-day Winter Frost Season egg hunt. Triggered by Naughty NPC on Onsen Mountain, this Onsen Egg Heartopia quest revolves around locating the Beige Cracked Egg (Day 1's gift egg) and photographing it. It's a treasure hunt blending exploration, photography, and seasonal fun.

details.title: Key Event Details
details.duration: Duration: Winter Frost Season (Jan 24 - Mar 14, 2026), daily eggs reset.
details.why: Why Hunt? Earn Festival Tokens for Azure's Fashionwave Store (winter outfits, furniture).

start.title: How to Start the Onsen Egg Promise Quest
start.prereq: Before chasing the Onsen Egg Heartopia, complete prerequisites:
start.step1: Enter Winter Frost Season (log in during event period).
start.step2: Teleport to Onsen Mountain (north map area).
start.step3: Complete Atara's opening ceremony (talk to Atara and Annie for cutscene and area unlock).
start.npc: Head to Naughty NPC (bus stop left uphill path).
start.interact: Interact: "The first gift egg shouldn't be far from Naughty. Find it and take a picture."
start.tracker: Quest icon (snowman or fashion event) appears on your map. Track progress (1/16). This setup ensures you're ready for the Onsen Egg Heartopia hunt!

location.title: Exact Onsen Egg Heartopia Location (Day 1 Beige Cracked Egg)
location.desc: The Onsen Egg Promise Heartopia egg spawns near Naughty—no wandering required! Here's the precise Onsen Egg Heartopia spot:
location.primary: Primary Location: Top of the fence on the long bridge platform (pathway from Naughty uphill).
location.alternate: Alternate Spots (confirmed by players): Right behind Naughty boy, ruins near three capybara statues, or small post by the fence overlooking the hot spring lake.

directions.title: From Naughty:
directions.step3: Spot the beige cracked egg perched on the wooden fence rail (glowing subtly in snow).

location.tip: Pro Tip: Use task tracker—egg highlights when close. If missed, visit friends' homes for Egg Ornament photos (furniture version).

steps.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Complete Onsen Egg Promise
steps.intro: Follow these for flawless Onsen Egg Heartopia completion:
steps.step2: Locate Egg: Head to fence (30-60 seconds walk).
steps.step3: Photograph: Open camera (default key: C or tap icon), frame the Onsen Egg Heartopia, snap!
steps.step5: Claim Rewards: Check quest log (progress updates).
steps.time: Total time: Under 2 minutes. Repeat daily for next Onsen Egg Promise variants.

rewards.title: Rewards for Onsen Egg Heartopia Quest
rewards.intro: Instant gratification from Onsen Egg Promise Heartopia:
rewards.ornament: Egg Ornament (placeable furniture—let friends photo yours!).
rewards.full: Full 16-egg haul: Onsen Egg Recipe (cookable dish) + exclusive winter cosmetics. Tokens buy at Azure's store (daily resets—visit often!).

tips.title: Tips, Tricks & Common Issues for Onsen Egg Promise
tips.cantFind: Can't Find Egg? Ensure quest accepted; restart game/server hop.
tips.server: Server Differences: SEA (UTC+7) spawns earliest; NA similar.
tips.missed: Missed Day 1? Use friends' ornaments or wait for reruns.
tips.efficiency: Max Efficiency: Combine with daily routines (feed animals nearby).
tips.future: Future Days: Follow our All Egg Locations guide (updating daily).

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is the Onsen Egg Promise a permanent quest?
faq.a1: No, the Onsen Egg Promise is part of the Winter Frost Season event (Jan 24 - Mar 14, 2026). While you can collect rewards during this period, the quest will end when the season concludes.
faq.q2: Can I help a friend with their Onsen Egg Heartopia hunt?
faq.a2: Yes! You can guide friends to the location, but each player must photograph their own egg to complete the quest. Alternatively, friends can photograph your Egg Ornament furniture if you've placed it in your home.
faq.q3: What happens if I miss Day 1?
faq.a3: Don't worry! You can still participate in Days 2-16. If you missed the Day 1 egg, visit friends' homes to photograph their Egg Ornament, or wait for potential event reruns.

onsenEggAllLocations.metaDesc: Day 16 final! Heartopia Onsen Egg at Fashionwave igloo village wooden ramp; bubble near capybara feeder. Full 16-day location guide and rewards.
