onsenEggAllLocations.lastUpdated: Last Update: February 15, 2026 - Day 16 Finale Egg added!
onsenEggAllLocations.heroTitle: Heartopia Onsen Egg Locations: 16-Day Event Guide (Day 16 Final Day)
onsenEggAllLocations.heroDesc: Welcome to the most ambitious collection challenge in the Heartopia Winter Event! Track every Heartopia Onsen Egg location and claim your exclusive rewards.
onsenEggAllLocations.intro: The Heartopia Onsen Egg locations treasure hunt is a 16-day marathon that rewards the most dedicated explorers with exclusive winter-themed furniture and rare titles. Whether you are a seasoned decorator or a new citizen, finding every single Heartopia Onsen Egg location is the only way to claim the grand prize at the end of the season.
onsenEggAllLocations.introSecondary: This page is your central hub for the event. We update this guide every 24 hours to ensure you never miss a day. If you are just starting, be sure to check our Heartopia Home Page for basic gameplay mechanics and winter prep tips.

howWorks.title: How the Heartopia Onsen Egg Hunt Works
howWorks.desc: Unlike previous gathering quests, the Heartopia Onsen Egg locations hunt requires a two-step verification process every day. Simply finding the egg is not enough to add it to your permanent collection.
howWorks.step1Desc: Locate the physical egg at its daily spawn point and take a clear picture using the in-game camera. Ensure the egg is highlighted in your viewfinder.
howWorks.step2Desc: After the photo, you must find a corresponding 'Snowman Bubble' floating in a different part of the map. Collecting this bubble officially 'claims' the egg for your inventory.
howWorks.trackingDesc: You can check your status by opening your collection menu and selecting the 'Onsen Egg Promised Treasure Hunt' tab. A checkmark will appear next to the specific egg once both the photo and the bubble have been secured.

tracker.title: Daily Heartopia Onsen Egg & Snowman Bubble Tracker
tracker.subtitle: Stay on track with our day-by-day breakdown of the Heartopia Winter Event. Remember, you must take a photo of the egg first, then hunt down the specific bubble to claim your prize!
tracker.day1Title: Day 1: The First Step at Onsen Mountain
tracker.day1Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to the Onsen Mountain Peak. You will find the first egg resting near the heat of the main steam vent.
tracker.day1Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: Travel back toward the center of civilization at the Town Square Fountain. The snowman bubble is floating in the air near the Mayor’s House.
tracker.day2Title: Day 2: The White Cracked Egg & The Hidden Pond
tracker.day2Egg: Egg Photo Location: The second Heartopia Onsen Egg location is at the Winter Festival area at the very top of your map. Look for the white cracked egg sitting between the cooking and gardening vendors near the ice skating rink.
tracker.day2Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: This bubble is a bit more hidden. Navigate to the Residential Pond area. It is tucked between Garden Street and Residential Street, floating just above the water's surface.
tracker.quickTip: Quick Tip: If you’re struggling with the climb, use the bus stop teleport to save time.
tracker.visualCue: Visual Cue: The bubble is located near the bridge by Bob's Shop and the Pet Store.
tracker.day3Title: Day 3: The Green Cracked Egg & The Igloo Village
tracker.day3Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to the Fashionwave Festival Hub. Look for the 'Igloo Village' and find the middle igloo with wooden stairs. The green cracked egg is hidden directly under the stairs in the snow.
tracker.day3Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: For Day 3, the Snowman Bubble appears immediately after taking the photo! Look for the glow right next to the stairs.
tracker.day4Title: Day 4: The Cyan Cracked Egg & Blanc's Flower Stall
tracker.day4Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to the snowy Fashionwave Event area on Onsen Mountain. Find Blanc's flower stall (flower van). The Cyan Cracked Egg is hidden in a greenish basket tucked right behind the stall next to rocks and blue flower pots.
tracker.day4Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: After taking the photo, the Egg Ornament bubble will spawn. Check the nearby area or head to the Deer Tower NW (lamppost by red flowers/pond) or the Spirit Oak forest.
tracker.day5Title: Day 5: Red Cracked Egg & Photo Shooting Spot
tracker.day5Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to the Onsen Mountain area. Look specifically for the 'Photo Shooting' zone near the mountain peak. The Red Cracked Egg is hidden in between the camera equipment and the flower bushes.
tracker.day5Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: After taking the photo, the Snowman Bubble (Egg Ornament) will appear nearby. Search the immediate area around the photo spot or check the path leading towards the Spirit Oak forest.
tracker.day6Title: Day 6: Cracked Egg on Snowman Dome / Igloo Top
tracker.day6Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to the Fashionwave Event area on Onsen Mountain. Look for the snowman dome top near the skating rink, igloo roof, or mini slide in the igloo village area. The Cracked Egg is sitting on top of the dome/igloo edge.
tracker.day6Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: After taking the photo, head to House Lot #4. The Snowman Bubble (Egg Ornament) is floating near the middle pole/street sign near the big tree.
tracker.day7Title: Day 7: Black Cracked Egg at Atara's Igloo
tracker.day7Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to Fashionwave Event area on Onsen Mountain. Navigate to the igloo village and enter Atara's Igloo (Mayor Atara's ice house). The Black Cracked Egg is sitting on the center table inside. This is an easy indoor find - no climbing required!
tracker.day7Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: After taking the photo, fast travel to Art Street. The Snowman Bubble is floating near the Rainbow Bridge. Pop it to claim your Day 7 Black Egg Ornament furniture.
tracker.day8Title: Day 8: Red Cracked Egg at Winter Utopia
tracker.day8Egg: Egg Photo Location: Head to Winter Utopia area. Look for the coffee umbrella on the right side of the igloo. The Red Cracked Egg is perched on top of the coffee umbrella. This location requires some careful positioning to spot the egg!
tracker.day8Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: After taking the photo, fast travel to Fishing Village. The Snowman Bubble is floating in front of the red barn at the top of the fishing shop. Pop it to claim your Day 8 Red Egg Ornament furniture.
tracker.day9Title: Day 9: Orange Egg in Onsen Mountain Desert
tracker.day9Egg: Egg Location: Head to the Southeast Desert area of Onsen Mountain. Look for a large tan rock on the sand (ground level). The Orange Egg is next to it, surrounded by red desert plants and green palm-like bushes. A wooden structure is visible in the background.
tracker.day9Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: Go to the Fishing Village (Zephyr Sea) Wooden Pier. The bubble is floating above the water near the vertical fishing net pole, between the pier and the small island. Simply jump from the pier edge to collect it.
tracker.day10Title: Day 10: Yellow Cracked Egg at Blanc's Stall
tracker.day10Egg: Egg Location: Head to the Fashionwave Event area. Find Blanc's stall (the vendor). The Yellow Cracked Egg is right on the right side of the stall, behind Blanc. No jumping needed, it's directly visible.
tracker.day10Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: Go to Garden Street, in front of Blanc's house. From the heart-shaped archway towards the building with the rose sign, the bubble is in front of the sign by the roof.
tracker.day11Title: Day 11: Emerald Cracked Egg at Winter Utopia
tracker.day11Egg: Egg Location: Head to Winter Utopia between the palm trees in front of Massimo, or go to Onsen Mountain near House Lot 8 at Stone Cliff. The Emerald/Green Cracked Egg is on the ground, no jumping needed.
tracker.day11Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: Onsen area, near the egg location or around House Lot 8 Stone Cliff. Look for the snow bubble to collect the Emerald Ornament furniture.
tracker.day12Title: Day 12: Aquamarine Cracked Egg at Winter Utopia
tracker.day12Egg: Egg Location: Head to the Onsen region [Winter Utopia site] near Azure NPC. Go to the left side of the rock near the Azure NPC, and you will find the 12th-day egg behind the plant.
tracker.day12Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: Go to the Suburban Lake region, behind Andrew NPC. Once you reach where Andrew is standing, go behind him and find two big trees with a hanging signboard. The 12th-day furniture bubble is floating around this tree.
tracker.day13Title: Day 13: Blue Cracked Egg at Crater Lake Fish Stall
tracker.day13Egg: Egg Location: Head to the top left corner of Crater Lake on Onsen Mountain. Look for the Frostpore fish event icon. The Blue Cracked Egg is glowing on the roof of the fish stall, right above the signboard. You'll need to climb up to capture the photo.
tracker.day13Bubble: Corresponding Bubble Location: Head to Amethyst Beach. The purple furniture bubble is floating around the Lifeguard Tower.
tracker.day14Title: Day 14: Purple Cracked Egg at Massimo's Stall
tracker.day14Egg: Egg Location: Final purple cracked egg at the very top of Massimo's Cooking Stall / Winter Frost stall roof in the Fashionwave event area. From the Winter Utopia bus stop, head straight toward Massimo's stall and climb to the highest roof edge. Variants have been reported behind Naughty boy at the Onsen entrance, near the sculptures in front of the ice skating rink, under the stairs leading to the event area, or behind the rock next to NPC Azura (west beach/Fashionwave). Look for a strong purple glow from the ground as a visual cue.
tracker.day14Bubble: Corresponding Bubble / Furniture Location: Primary Snowman Bubble at the Azure Fashionwave Store entrance (front door and sign area, floating bubble). Some players also report sightings near the ice skating rink sculptures or Naughty area. Reward: Day 14 Purple Egg Ornament furniture. Place it in your home for friends to take photos and complete their collections. Respawn typically occurs 1–5 minutes after the egg photo; try relogging if the bubble does not appear.
tracker.day15Title: Day 15: Rosy Cracked Egg (Finale) at Ice Rink
tracker.day15Egg: Egg Location: The grand finale Rosy Cracked Egg is located at the north end of the Winter Utopia ice rink. Look for the colorful flags and the wooden bench; the egg is resting on top of the bench overlooking the frozen lake.
tracker.day15Bubble: Corresponding Bubble / Furniture Location: After taking the photo, travel to the windmill area nearby at Winter Utopia. The final furniture bubble is floating near the ferret's water trough and the flower beds. Pop it to claim your Rosy Egg Ornament and complete your 15-day collection!
tracker.day16Title: Day 16: Finale Egg at Fashionwave Igloo Village
tracker.day16Egg: Egg Location: Near the igloo beside the wooden ramp at the top of the small stone in the Onsen Mountain Fashionwave igloo village. Climb the wooden ramp to the small stone top—egg perched on the edge. Variants: behind Naughty at Onsen entrance, sculptures in front of ice skating rink, under stairs to event area. Naughty NPC quest → snowman map icon to igloo village → wooden ramp/small stone top (egg glow visible from ground). Difficulty ★★☆☆.
tracker.day16Bubble: Bubble / Furniture Location: Primary spot near the capybara feeder at Onsen Mountain (floating bubble by trough/feeder). Variants: Art Street or Azure store entrance. Pop for Day 16 Finale Ornament (exhibition furniture). Respawns 1–5 mins after egg photo; relog or server hop if missing.

times.title: Important Update Times & Tips
times.reset: Daily Reset: The Heartopia Winter Event daily egg resets at 00:00 Server Time.
times.variations: Bubble Variations: Be aware that 'Snowman Bubbles' for the egg hunt look different from standard furniture bubbles. They typically have a more crystalline glow.
times.limited: Limited Time: This event lasts exactly 16 days. If you miss an egg, it may be impossible to claim the final achievement once the Heartopia Winter Event concludes.

faq.title: Heartopia Winter Event FAQ
faq.q1: Can I find all 16 eggs in one day?
faq.a1: No. The Heartopia Onsen Egg locations are time-gated. Only one specific egg and its corresponding bubble appear each day for the 16-day duration.
faq.q2: What if I take the photo but forget the bubble?
faq.a2: Your progress will not be saved. You must collect the Snowman Bubble on the same day you take the photo to count that Heartopia Onsen Egg location as completed.
faq.q3: What is the grand reward for the 16-day hunt?
faq.a3: While the developers have kept it a surprise, leaks from the Heartopia Winter Event community suggest it is a permanent 'Winter Guardian' statue and a unique player frame.
faq.q4: What if I missed a previous day's egg?
faq.a4: No problem! You can still complete your collection. Simply visit a friend's house who has already placed that day's 'Egg Ornament' furniture. Take a photo of their ornament to count it towards your event progress.

extraBubbles.title: Heartopia Winter Event: All 10 Extra Snowman Bubble Locations
extraBubbles.desc: Besides the daily 'Quest Egg Bubbles', there are 10 extra Snowman Bubbles scattered across the map. Collecting them grants limited furniture, building materials, and concert props.

list.name: Onsen Mountain Bus Stop
list.pos: Located on top of the Onsen Mountain Bus Stop building, directly behind the Gardening Vendor.

list.name: Old Sea Concert Portal
list.pos: Located in the Old Sea area at the top of the map, near the portal leading to the Snow Opening Concert venue.

list.name: Garden Street Heart Arch
list.pos: On top of a heart-shaped arch at the river intersection in Garden Street.

list.name: Residential Street Rooftop
list.pos: On the roof of a house on Residential Street, to the right of the Suburban Lake.

list.name: Forest Lake Waterfall Tent
list.pos: In the Forest Lake area on the right side of the map, near the tent and campfire by the waterfall.

list.name: Forest Lake Bridge
list.pos: Above the wooden bridge crossing Forest Lake in the bottom right of the map. The bubble rotates along the bridge structure.

list.name: Fishing Village East Pier
list.pos: On the roof of a barn-like building at the East Pier of the Fishing Village in the Zephyr Sea area.

list.name: Lighthouse
list.pos: Right in front of the Lighthouse at the bottom of the map. Reach it by jumping on the nearby rocks or structures.

list.name: Meadow Lake Swing Set
list.pos: On top of the swing set by the Meadow Lake pond on the far left of the map.

list.name: Whale Mountain Structure
list.pos: Behind the building structure on the far left, right of Whale Sea, near a large tree.

points.title: Building Materials

snowConcert.metaTitle: Heartopia Snow Concert Guide: Time, Location & Rewards
snowConcert.metaDesc: Join the Heartopia Snow Concert! 7PM server time converter, Fashionwave location map, glow sticks, quest steps, and reward guide. Don't miss out!
snowConcert.heroTitle: Heartopia Snow Concert Guide: Time, Location, Quest & Rewards
snowConcert.heroDesc: Experience the ultimate winter party! Master the Snow Concert schedule, find the best viewing spots, and claim exclusive seasonal rewards in Heartopia.
snowConcert.intro: The Snow Concert is a daily, limited-time musical event in Heartopia during the Winter Frost Season. Located at the Onsen Mountain concert venue, it's the perfect place to hang out with friends, listen to cozy tunes, and earn exclusive rewards like Festival Tokens and winter furniture.

timeSection.title: Snow Concert Time & Schedule (Server Time)
timeSection.desc: The daily concert starts at **7:00 PM Server Time (UTC-5)**. To complete the related quest, we recommend joining within the first 20 minutes.
timeSection.weeklyNote: Weekly Schedule: Daily during the first and last weeks of the season. During middle weeks, concerts are held on weekends only.

locationSection.title: Exact Location & How to Enter
locationSection.content: The concert takes place at the **Fashionwave Concert Venue** on Onsen Mountain. Look for the large, colorful stage to the right of the ice skating rink. To enter the restricted concert area, you must have **Glow Sticks** equipped.

questSteps.title: Snow Concert Quest Steps
steps: Talk to NPC Atara near the Fashionwave entrance to accept the Snow Concert Quest.
Purchase Glow Sticks (costing 20-50 Festival Tokens) from the nearby souvenir stall.
Enter the venue during the first 20 minutes of the show (between 7:00 PM and 7:20 PM Server Time).
Wave your glow sticks, watch the fireworks, and dance with fellow Heartopia residents.
The quest will complete automatically, and rewards will be sent to your in-game mailbox.

list.solution: Ensure your device clock matches the 7:00 PM Server Time window. Try relogging to refresh the event instance.
Make sure you have purchased and equipped Glow Sticks. Access is usually limited to the first 20 minutes for quest completion.
Talk to Atara again after the concert ends. If it still fails, check if you completed the required interaction time (approx. 10 mins).

rewards.title: Concert Rewards: What's in it for you?
list: Exclusive Glow Stick furniture and fireworks-themed decorations.
Progress toward the 'Fashionwave Socialite' limited-time achievements.

tips.title: Pro Tips for the Best Experience
list: Arrive at least 5 minutes early to avoid server queue issues.
Bring friends! The Snow Concert is an 'Atmos-enabled' social event—the more residents, the better the fireworks.
Keep an eye on the weather; clear skies make for the best photo opportunities.

items.q: What is 7PM server time in Heartopia?
items.a: Heartopia uses UTC-5 as server time. This is 8:00 AM next day for players in China (UTC+8) and 7:00 PM for players on the US East Coast.

items.q: Can I join the concert late?
items.a: While the concert lasts 2 hours, you must join during the 'Opening Window' (first 20 mins) to complete the daily quest and earn rewards.

items.q: Where do I buy Glow Sticks?
items.a: Glow Sticks are sold by the Festival Vendor at the entrance of the Fashionwave venue on Onsen Mountain.

about.content:
Heartopia Hub is a fan-made community website dedicated to providing the most comprehensive and high-quality guides for Heartopia. Our mission is to help players discover every secret, master every recipe, and make the most of their cozy life in the game.

We are a group of passionate players who believe that games like Heartopia provide a much-needed 'healing' experience. Whether you're looking for the best fishing spots, housing inspiration, or NPC gifts, we've got you covered.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.content:
At Heartopia Hub, accessible from theheartopia.com, one of our main priorities is the privacy of our visitors. This Privacy Policy document contains types of information that is collected and recorded by Heartopia Hub and how we use it.

### Log Files
Heartopia Hub follows a standard procedure of using log files. These files log visitors when they visit websites. All hosting companies do this and a part of hosting services' analytics. The information collected by log files include internet protocol (IP) addresses, browser type, Internet Service Provider (ISP), date and time stamp, referring/exit pages, and possibly the number of clicks.

### Cookies and Web Beacons
Like any other website, Heartopia Hub uses 'cookies'. These cookies are used to store information including visitors' preferences, and the pages on the website that the visitor accessed or visited.

### Google DoubleClick DART Cookie
Google is one of a third-party vendor on our site. It also uses cookies, known as DART cookies, to serve ads to our site visitors based upon their visit to theheartopia.com and other sites on the internet.

### Our Advertising Partners
Some of advertisers on our site may use cookies and web beacons. Our advertising partners include Google AdSense.

### Privacy Policies
You may consult this list to find the Privacy Policy for each of the advertising partners of Heartopia Hub.

### Third Party Privacy Policies
Heartopia Hub's Privacy Policy does not apply to other advertisers or websites. Thus, we are advising you to consult the respective Privacy Policies of these third-party ad servers for more detailed information.

### Children's Information
Another part of our priority is adding protection for children while using the internet. We encourage parents and guardians to observe, participate in, and/or monitor and guide their online activity.

### Consent
By using our website, you hereby consent to our Privacy Policy and agree to its Terms and Conditions.

auroraGuide.metaTitle: Heartopia Aurora Weather Guide: Join the Aurora Banquet
auroraGuide.metaDesc: Discover Heartopia aurora weather secrets! Learn to predict the aurora sky, find the Aurora Banquet location, and claim your exclusive event rewards today!
auroraGuide.heroTitle: Heartopia Aurora Weather Guide: How to Join the Aurora Banquet
auroraGuide.heroDesc: The winter season has arrived with a magical glow! Predict the sky, join the festivities, and claim rare rewards.
auroraGuide.intro: The winter season has arrived with a magical glow! Currently, the most searched mystery in the community is the Heartopia aurora weather and its elusive connection to the nightly festivities. Seeing the sky light up with neon greens and purples is not just a visual treat; it is the prerequisite for the Aurora Banquet Heartopia, a time-limited social event that offers some of the rarest rewards in the 2026 Winter Event.
auroraGuide.intro2: In this guide, we will teach you how to read the sky, predict the Heartopia aurora weather, and ensure you never miss an Aurora Banquet Heartopia gathering again.
auroraGuide.predictDesc: Unlike rain or snow, Heartopia aurora weather is a rare atmospheric phenomenon that only occurs during specific window periods in the winter update.
auroraGuide.forecastDesc: The most reliable way to track Heartopia aurora weather is through your in-game smartphone or smartwatch. Look specifically for the "Glowing Wave" icon. If this icon appears in the 6:00 PM to 6:00 AM slot, you are guaranteed to witness the aurora that night.
auroraGuide.visualDesc: If you forget to check your watch, keep an eye on the horizon starting at 6:00 PM server time. The transition to Heartopia aurora weather begins with a slight purple tint in the clouds before the full green bands manifest. Once the colors are vibrant, the Aurora Banquet Heartopia will officially be active in the festival zones.
auroraGuide.aboutBanquetTitle: Everything About the Aurora Banquet Heartopia
auroraGuide.aboutBanquetDesc: Once the sky confirms the Heartopia aurora weather, the Aurora Banquet Heartopia begins. This is a community-based event where players gather to celebrate under the stars.
auroraGuide.location1: Onsen Mountain Festival Hub: Near the large campfires where Doris often visits.
auroraGuide.location2: The Old Sea Concert Grounds: Near the portal where the winter opening concert is held.
auroraGuide.location3: The banquet remains active as long as the Heartopia aurora weather persists, typically ending at 6:00 AM the following morning.
auroraGuide.activitiesDesc: To participate in the Aurora Banquet Heartopia, you should bring specific food items to share with other players. Popular choices include the Iced Latte and Frosted Pancakes, which are also currently trending in the winter update. Sharing food during the Aurora Banquet Heartopia increases your social affinity and unlocks "event energy".
auroraGuide.rewardsTitle: Rewards for Frequenting the Aurora Banquet
auroraGuide.rewardsDesc: Why is everyone chasing the Heartopia aurora weather? The rewards at the Aurora Banquet Heartopia are essential for winter progression.
auroraGuide.item1: Glow Sticks: Essential for cheering during the winter concerts.
auroraGuide.item2: Penguin Material Packs: Rare building materials used to craft the new winter-themed wall sets.
auroraGuide.item3: Music Note Fireworks: A celebratory item often rewarded for group interactions during the banquet.
auroraGuide.hiddenDesc: Witnessing the Heartopia aurora weather while sitting at a banquet table with five or more friends will unlock the "Celestial Guest" achievement. This is one of the most prestigious badges of the 2026 season.
auroraGuide.tipsTitle: Tips for the Ultimate Aurora Experience
auroraGuide.tip1: Photo Mode: The Heartopia aurora weather provides the best lighting for photography. Use the "Long Exposure" filter in your game camera to make the colors pop.
auroraGuide.tip2: Stay Warm: Being in the mountains during Heartopia aurora weather can drain your stamina faster due to the cold. Bring an Iced Drink or a hot meal to keep your energy high.
auroraGuide.tip3: Group Up: The Aurora Banquet Heartopia is more rewarding when played in a group. Use the "World Chat" to find which instance has the most active banquet.
auroraGuide.faqQ1: Does the Aurora appear every night?
auroraGuide.faqA1: No. The Heartopia aurora weather is a random event, though its frequency is increased during the "Deep Winter" phase of the update.
auroraGuide.faqQ2: What if I can't find the Aurora Banquet?
auroraGuide.faqA2: Even if the Heartopia aurora weather is active, you must be in a major social hub like Onsen Mountain or the Winter Festival area to see the Aurora Banquet Heartopia NPCs and tables.
auroraGuide.faqQ3: Are the rewards from the banquet permanent?
auroraGuide.faqA3: The materials like the Penguin Material Packs are permanent, but the banquet itself is a seasonal event that will end with the winter update.

frostedPancake.metaTitle: Frosted Pancake Recipe Heartopia: 5-Star Cooking Guide
frostedPancake.metaDesc: Master the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia! Discover ingredients, ratios, and where to unlock this 5-star winter dish for the Aurora Banquet today.
frostedPancake.heroTitle: Frosted Pancake Recipe Heartopia: The Ultimate 5-Star Cooking Guide
frostedPancake.heroDesc: Master the gold standard of winter cooking. Essential for the Aurora Banquet and high-stamina mountain exploration.
frostedPancake.intro: In the chilly world of the 2026 Winter Event, one dish has become the gold standard for survival and celebration: the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia. Whether you are preparing for the Aurora Banquet Heartopia or need a high-stamina snack for your Heartopia onsen egg location hunt, mastering this recipe is a must for every top-tier chef.
frostedPancake.introSecondary: In this guide, we break down the exact ingredients, the best ratios for high-quality results, and where to unlock the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia today.

unlock.title: How to Unlock the Frosted Pancake Recipe Heartopia
unlock.desc: Unlike basic fishing recipes, the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia is a seasonal specialty. You cannot find it by just experimenting at the stove without the blueprint first.
unlock.whereDesc: To start cooking, head to the Winter Festival area at the top of the map. Visit the Food Vendor NPC located next to the ice skating rink. You can purchase the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia using event-exclusive currency or gold.

ingredients.title: The Perfect Frosted Pancake Recipe Heartopia Ingredients
ingredients.desc: To achieve a 5-star rating, you need a balance of sweetness and substance. Here is the most efficient ratio discovered by the community for the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia:
ingredients.twist: Winter Twist: 1x Winter Berry (Limited Time Ingredient)
ingredients.banquetTitle: Cooking for the Aurora Banquet Heartopia
ingredients.banquetDesc: If you are making these for the Aurora Banquet Heartopia, adding a rare variant of the berry will increase the "Event Energy" you provide to the group, helping you unlock rewards like Penguin Material Packs faster.

benefits.title: Why You Need the Frosted Pancake Recipe Heartopia
benefits.desc: Why is the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia trending so heavily? It offers two massive benefits for the current update:
benefits.benefit1Desc: Eating a Frosted Pancake grants you a "Frost Shield" buff, allowing you to stay on the snowy peaks of Onsen Mountain longer without losing stamina.
benefits.benefit2Desc: This is the highest-rated "shareable" food for the Aurora Banquet Heartopia. Sharing a stack of these is the fastest way to earn the "Celestial Guest" achievement.

tips.title: Pro Tips for Heartopia Chefs
tips.tip1Desc: Since the Heartopia aurora weather is unpredictable, keep a stack of at least 10 Frosted Pancakes in your inventory.
tips.tip2Desc: Pair your pancakes with an Iced Latte (another Breakout recipe!) for the ultimate stamina recovery combo.

faq.title: Frosted Pancake Recipe Heartopia FAQ
faq.q1: Can I make Frosted Pancakes without the Winter Berry?
faq.a1: You can substitute the berry with a standard fruit, but you will only get a "Standard Pancake," which does not provide the same cold resistance as the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia specialty.
faq.q2: Does the quality of the Milk matter?
faq.a2: Yes! Using high-quality milk increases the duration of the stamina-regen buff provided by the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia.

recipesGuide.intro: Cooking is one of the most rewarding hobbies in Heartopia, offering players a way to restore stamina, survive harsh weather, and bond with friends during social events. With the latest 2026 Winter Update, several new Heartopia recipes have surfaced, becoming essential for those participating in the Aurora Banquet and the Onsen Egg hunt.
recipesGuide.introSecondary: This comprehensive Heartopia cooking guide covers everything from basic ingredients to the most advanced 5-star seasonal delicacies.

winterSection.title: Featured Winter Event Recipes (Trending Now)
winterSection.desc: These are the most searched Heartopia recipes in the past 24 hours. If you are active in the Winter Event, make sure you have these unlocked and stocked in your inventory.
winterSection.pancakeDesc: The gold standard for the current season. Provides a Frost Shield buff vital for exploring Onsen Mountain.
winterSection.pancakeIng: Ingredients: 2x Wheat, 1x Milk, 1x Sugar, 1x Winter Berry.
winterSection.pancakeBest: Best For: Aurora Banquet sharing and Cold Resistance.
winterSection.drinksDesc: When your stamina is low from hunting for Heartopia Onsen Egg locations, these drinks are your best friend.
winterSection.latte: Iced Latte: 1x Coffee Bean, 1x Milk, 1x Ice Cube.
winterSection.drink: Iced Drink: 2x Fruit (any), 1x Ice Cube, 1x Sugar.

howWorks.title: How Cooking Works in Heartopia
howWorks.desc: Before you can master the Heartopia recipes, you need to understand the cooking mechanics.
howWorks.blueprintsDesc: While some basic dishes can be discovered through experimentation, rare ones like the Frosted Pancake recipe Heartopia must be purchased from vendors at the Winter Festival.
howWorks.qualityDesc: Using high-quality or 'Shiny' versions of ingredients like Milk or Wheat will increase the star rating of the final dish, extending the duration of its buffs.
howWorks.kitchenDesc: Ensure your house has a stove or an oven installed through the Housing menu to start practicing your Heartopia cooking guide techniques.

categories.title: Permanent Recipe Categories
categories.desc: Beyond the winter season, these Heartopia recipes provide year-round benefits for fishing, gardening, and socializing.
categories.fishSoup: Spicy Fish Soup: 1x Rare Fish, 1x Chili, 1x Water. (Increases rare catch chance for 5 mins).

tips.title: Tips for Efficient Cooking
tips.bulkDesc: Always cook in stacks of 5 or 10. Especially for the Aurora Banquet Heartopia to gain maximum affinity.
tips.dorisDesc: Traveling merchant Doris sells rare seasonings. Adding these boosts dishes to 4 or 5 stars instantly.
tips.farmDesc: Growing Wheat and Sugar Cane in your garden is much cheaper than buying from the Town Shop.

faq.title: Heartopia Cooking & Recipes FAQ
faq.q1: Can I fail a recipe?
faq.a1: Yes. If you use a wrong combination of ingredients, you will produce 'Questionable Food,' which restores very little stamina and provides no buffs. Always follow a verified Heartopia cooking guide.
faq.q2: Where do I get Ice Cubes for winter drinks?
faq.a2: Ice Cubes can be gathered from the Ice Skating Rink area or purchased from the Food Vendor at the Winter Festival during Heartopia aurora weather events.
faq.q3: How do I unlock 5-star recipes?
faq.a3: You need to reach Cooking Level 10 and use at least two high-quality (Blue or Purple tier) ingredients in your Heartopia recipes.

faq.q1: Where can I find the full Heartopia recipe list in-game?

faq.q2: Why did my cooking fail?

icedDrink.metaTitle: Heartopia Iced Drink Recipe: Stamina Boost Guide
icedDrink.metaDesc: Master the iced drink recipe in Heartopia! Learn Iced Latte & Coffee ingredients, Penguin Stove locations, Aurora Banquet quest unlock, and stamina tips.
icedDrink.heroTitle: Heartopia Iced Drink Recipe: Stamina Boost Guide
icedDrink.heroDesc: If you're currently hunting for {linkOnsen}, you already know the struggle of running out of energy halfway up the mountain. The iced drink Heartopia recipe is the most effective solution in the 2026 Winter Frost Event to keep your character moving without constant rest breaks.
icedDrink.updateNote: ⚡ Updated February 4, 2026 — This complete iced drink Heartopia guide covers Iced Latte, Iced Coffee, and all variants. Includes Penguin Stove locations, Massimo's Aurora Banquet Quest, bug fixes, and 5-star cooking tips!
icedDrink.proTipTitle: Pro Tip: Fruit Iced Drink Variants
icedDrink.proTipDesc: Swap Coffee Beans for 2x Fruit + 1x Ice Cube to create refreshing variants (Apple, Blueberry, Winter Berry). These provide +18-25 energy and unique movement speed buffs!

what.title: What is the Iced Drink in Heartopia?
what.desc: The iced drink Heartopia refers to event-exclusive beverages unlocked via the Aurora Banquet Quest. Talk to Massimo at the Onsen Mountain bistro during the Fashionwave/Winter Frost Event. He requests 1 iced drink + {linkPancake} for the festival feast.
what.fact1: Stove Required: Penguin Stove (event craft via materials; also at Massimo kitchen/ice rink ovens)
what.fact4: Unlock: Quest progress + learn at Penguin Stove (bug: may vanish—fix below)
what.rewardsDesc: Completing the Aurora Banquet Quest unlocks: Steak with Mashed White Radish, Creamy White Radish Soup recipes + Aurora Banquet display furniture for your home!

howTo.title: How to Make Iced Drink Heartopia (Step-by-Step)
howTo.step1Desc: Visit Onsen Mountain → Talk to Massimo at the bistro (Aurora Banquet Quest triggers automatically during Winter Frost Event)
howTo.step2Desc: Check your mailbox after dialogue or craft it with event materials. Alternative: Use stoves at Massimo's kitchen or the ice rink
howTo.step3Desc: Visit Massimo's cooking store: Frosting (50 Gold), Coffee Beans, Milk (affordable prices)
howTo.step4Desc: Select recipe → Start cooking mini-game: Tap circles to adjust heat (keep gauge in green zone, avoid empty/full)
howTo.step5Desc: Bring completed iced drink + Frosted Pancakes to Massimo → Unlock rewards!
howTo.miniGameTip: 🎯 Mini-Game Tips: Keep medium heat steady. ★★★★ rating needs precise taps for +28 Energy max!

where.title: Where to Get Iced Drink Ingredients & Stove
where.frostingDesc: 📍 Massimo's cooking store at Onsen Mountain Lake bistro (50 Gold)
where.coffeeDesc: 📍 Massimo's store or harvest from trees near Research Lab
where.milkDesc: 📍 Massimo's store or squeeze cows at Onsen farms
where.proMapTip: 💡 Pro Map Tip: All ingredients and stoves are concentrated in the Onsen Mountain area. Fast travel to the {linkAurora} event marker for quick access!

strategic.title: Strategic Use: Iced Drinks & The Onsen Egg Hunt
strategic.intro: Why is iced drink Heartopia currently a "Breakout" keyword? Because of the altitude!
strategic.problemDesc: When navigating to {linkEggLocations}, the steep inclines of Onsen Mountain drain stamina 25% faster. Without iced drinks:
strategic.problem1: You need to rest 3 times before reaching the summit
strategic.problem2: Each rest takes 30-45 seconds of real time
strategic.problem3: Total climb time: 8-10 minutes per egg location
strategic.solution1: Drink 1 Iced Latte before climbing → instant +28 energy
strategic.solution2: Reach the Onsen Egg location in a single sprint
strategic.loadoutDesc: Carry a stack of at least 5 Iced Drinks before leaving your home for the {linkOnsenQuest} quest. Drink one right before you start the climb behind the waterfall!

bugs.title: Common Bugs & Fixes for Iced Drink Heartopia
bugs.bug1Desc: Fix: Reset game / Retalk to Massimo / Check Collections app (Gourmet Life section). This is a common post-quest bug reported on Feb 3, 2026.
bugs.bug2Desc: Fix: If mailbox Penguin Stove fails, use the ice rink ovens or Massimo's kitchen stove as alternatives.
bugs.bug3Desc: Fix: Frosting and some ingredients are event-limited. Buy from Massimo during Winter Frost Event only.

faq.title: Iced Drink Heartopia FAQ
faq.q1: Can I make Iced Drinks without the recipe in Heartopia?
faq.a1: You can try to 'Experiment' at the Penguin Stove, but without the blueprint unlocked from the Aurora Banquet Quest, there is a 50% chance of creating 'Questionable Food' instead of a proper iced drink. It's recommended to complete the quest first for guaranteed results.
faq.q2: What is the difference between Iced Drink and Iced Latte in Heartopia?
faq.a2: Iced Latte (Frosting + Coffee Bean + Milk x2) provides +20-28 energy and sells for 280-560 Gold. Iced Coffee (Frosting + Coffee Bean x3) has similar stats but uses more coffee for a stronger flavor. Fruit Iced Drinks (Frosting + Ice Cube + Fruit x2) provide movement speed boosts instead of pure stamina.
faq.a5: Completing the quest by delivering iced drinks and Frosted Pancakes to Massimo unlocks advanced recipes: Steak with Mashed White Radish, Creamy White Radish Soup, plus Aurora Banquet display furniture for your home decoration.

frostsporeButterfliesGuide.catchStep5: Collect 4: Auto to Collections for quests.

faq.q1: Where can I find Winter Smew in Heartopia?
faq.a1: Winter Smew spawns at Forest Lake shore, Deer Tower Lake, and near houses 5-6 in suburban areas during the winter season. They prefer quieter lake sections and appear in any winter weather.
faq.q2: What level do I need for birdwatching?
faq.a2: You need Birdwatching Level 2 and a Level 2+ net to catch winter birds effectively. Complete basic scans at the Research Lab to unlock Level 2, then upgrade your net at the shop or lab.
faq.q3: How do I get 3-star bird info cards?
faq.a3: To get 3-star bird info cards, take photos when birds are feeding or preening. Maintain medium distance—too close will scare them, too far reduces clarity. Perfect timing and distance are key!
faq.q4: Can I trade duplicate bird info cards?
faq.a4: Yes! Visit Bailey J NPC to exchange duplicate bird info cards for gold coins and upgrade materials. This is a great way to monetize your extra cards.
faq.q5: Why isn't the Winter Mandarin Duck spawning?
faq.a5: The Winter Mandarin Duck is phase-locked — it only appears during the Extra Phase Photography stage of the Winter Frost event (Week 3). Make sure your Birdwatching Hobby is at least Level 2 and that you've completed the initial Birdwatching tasks with Bailey J. Once unlocked, head to Suburban Lake and look on the water surface. It can appear at any time of day or weather once the Extra Phase is active.

whatIs.title: What is Watering Crops in Heartopia?
whatIs.desc: Watering crops is a core Gardening Lv1 task: Use watering can on dry garden plots to make them sparkle (wet). Required for quests, crop growth, and Gourmet Life progression. Common issues include plots not counting, rain/snow blocking watering, and multi-water feature not working.

steps.title: Step-by-Step: How to Water Crops with Watering Can
steps.step1Desc: Open Toolbox (right side) → Select watering can icon → Equip.
steps.step2Desc: Walk near dry garden plot until sparkle indicator shows you're in range.
steps.step3Desc: Tap watering can icon (mobile) or right-click (PC) → Plot sparkles = success.
steps.step4Desc: Hold icon for 2-3 seconds (Lv3 unlocked) → Waters 3-5 adjacent plots at once.
steps.step5Desc: Quest log updates when sparkling plots are counted.

locations.desc: Primary gardens: Onsen Mountain farms, player houses, event areas.

bugs.title: Common Bugs & Fixes (Top Player Issues)
bugs.fix1: Water only dry plots; relog/quest restart
bugs.fix2: Wait for Sunny; use indoor house plots
bugs.fix4: Cook 10x basic recipes for Gardening Lv3

lv3.title: Lv3 Multi-Water Tips & Tricks
lv3.tip1: Hold icon 2-3 seconds → Waters line of 3-5 plots.
lv3.tip2: Best for farm rows; saves 70% time.
lv3.tip3: Works on adjacent plots in straight lines or clusters.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Why won't my sparkling plots count for the quest?
faq.a1: The quest only counts plots that were dry when you watered them. If a plot is already sparkling (wet), watering it again won't add to your quest progress. Try relogging or restarting the quest if the issue persists.
faq.q2: Can I water crops during rain or snow in Heartopia?
faq.a2: No, the watering can is disabled during rain and snow weather. You'll need to wait for sunny weather or use indoor house plots that aren't affected by weather.
faq.q3: How do I unlock the Lv3 multi-water feature?
faq.a3: Reach Gardening Level 3 by watering 50 plots total and completing Massimo's cow-related quests. Once unlocked, hold the watering can icon for 2-3 seconds to water 3-5 adjacent plots at once.
faq.q4: Where are the best locations to water crops for quests?
faq.a4: Onsen Farms have 20+ plots near Massimo's cows, making them ideal for both watering and milk quests. Player house lots (4-16 plots depending on upgrades) are also great for daily routines.
faq.q5: Why can't I water my crops?
faq.a5: Check two things: 1. Is your can empty? 2. Is the soil already dark (wet)?
faq.q6: Do I need to water in the rain?
faq.a6: No! Rain automatically waters all outdoor crops. Save your stamina for other tasks.
faq.a7: Automatic Sprinklers unlock at **Gardening Level 10** (craft at Blacksmith).

dogBreeds.metaTitle: Heartopia Dog Breeds: All 11 Breeds & Traits
dogBreeds.metaDesc: All 11 dog breeds in Heartopia — unlock Dog Caring, adopt your perfect pup, personality traits explained, training tricks, and every level reward.
dogBreeds.heroDesc: Everything about dogs in Heartopia — all 11 breeds, how to unlock Dog Caring, the daily adoption rotation, all 11 personality traits, training commands, and full level rewards. Updated February 2026.
dogBreeds.quote: The Golden Retriever secretly funds your island — bonded dogs bring back Gold Bags as daily gifts. Pair it with Seek Treasure and let your dog work for you.

unlock.title: How to Unlock the Dog Caring Hobby
unlock.intro: Dog Caring requires three prerequisites — they must all be met before dogs appear in the adoption pool.
unlock.step1: Reach Developer's Guild Member Level 12 by completing Resident Requests daily.
unlock.step2: Unlock and complete the Cat Caring hobby tutorial with Mrs. Joan first — it's the required prerequisite.
unlock.step3: Exchange one Hobby Expansion Ticket with Mrs. Joan at the Pet Adoption Center in town.
unlock.timeNote: ⏱ How long? Approximately 3–5 days of consistent play to reach D.G. Level 12. Cat Care unlocks before that, so you won't be waiting long.

adoption.title: How the Dog Adoption System Works
adoption.intro: Mrs. Joan shows 3 random dogs (and 3 random cats) every day. The selection resets at the daily server reset — there are no rerolls.
adoption.step1: Approach a dog at the adoption center and select Adopt.
adoption.step3: Name your dog — you can rename them later anytime.
adoption.step4: Your dog is sent directly to your home plot.
adoption.slotsTitle: How Many Dogs Can You Own?
adoption.warning: Dogs cannot be returned or rehomed. The only option is to board them for 20,000g — they remain part of your household permanently.
adoption.tipsTitle: Tips for Finding the Breed You Want
adoption.tip1: Visit the adoption center every day — the rotation is fully random.
adoption.tip2: Each dog's breed, traits, fur pattern, and eye color are shown on its ID card before adopting.
adoption.tip3: If your preferred breed appears, adopt immediately — it won't be there tomorrow.
adoption.tip4: Rare breeds appear more frequently during seasonal events.

breeds.title: All 11 Dog Breeds in Heartopia

appearance.title: Fur Colors, Patterns & Eye Variations

care.title: Daily Dog Care — How to Build Your Bond
care.intro: Bond level determines gift frequency and hobby XP speed. Monitor stats via the Pet app on your in-game watch.
care.feedDesc: Tap the food bowl icon to feed. Use dog food or snacks from your inventory. Sensitive dogs may refuse certain foods — experiment to find what works.
care.playDesc: Use toys to entertain your dog. Builds happiness faster than feeding alone.
care.batheDesc: Select Pet Wash when your dog is dirty. Follow the 3-stage process: wash → rinse → dry. A clean buff appears on your dog's profile.
care.trainDesc: Teach tricks using the Train option. Best hobby XP source and unlocks Doggie Time Collection rewards.
care.walkDesc: Take your dog out to explore town. If you walk too far ahead, they'll return home. Great for social encounters with other players.

training.title: All Dog Training Commands & Tricks
training.intro: Teaching tricks builds bond, earns Doggie Time Collection stars, and unlocks costumes and items not found anywhere else.

rows.effect: Dog searches for hidden items while you play — passive item discovery.
Dog offers paw on command — increases bond meter.
Dog shakes head on command — earns a Collection star.

training.howDesc: Select Train from your dog's interaction menu. Watch the move your dog performs, then applaud (correct) or reprimand (incorrect). Each session adds to the Doggie Time Collection.
training.tip: ⭐ Train Seek Treasure first — it's the only command with a real gameplay benefit, finding hidden items passively as you explore.

levels.title: Dog Caring Hobby — All Level Rewards
levels.intro: Earn Dog Caring XP through feeding, playing, bathing, and training. Spend Hobby Upgrade Tickets to level up.

rows.reward: 🐶 Dog Moment 1 (↑ High-Star Moment chance) · Dog Arrives! 1 — adopt your 1st dog
⚡ Energy Dog Food unlocked · Dog Arrives! 2 — adopt 2nd dog
🤖 Dog Moment 3 · Auto-Feed 1 — automatic feeder unlocked
🤖 Auto-Feed 2 (more feeders) · Dog Arrives! 3 — adopt 3rd dog
🎮 Dog Moment 6 · Come and Play action unlocked

levels.note: Key milestones: Level 5 for Auto-Feed (huge QoL — your dog won't starve while you're away), Level 4 for a 2nd dog, Level 8 for the 3rd dog slot.

tips.title: Pro Tips for Dog Owners
items: Adopt the day your favorite breed appears — even if traits aren't ideal. Breed is visual; traits are random on every dog.
Auto-Feed (Level 5) is the most important milestone — unlock it first. It removes daily feeding anxiety for multiple dogs.
Always teach Seek Treasure first. Every other trick is cosmetic — this one finds items for you in the background.
Bond = gifts. The higher your bond level, the more often your dog brings you random items and Gold Bags.
Do NOT board unless necessary. Boarding costs 20,000g and the dog stays in your household — there's no permanent release.
Heterochromia dogs (two different eye colors) are rare and worth keeping even if the breed isn't your top choice.
Check the adoption center every day — even when you already have 3 dogs. Tracking rare breeds helps complete the Doggie Time Collection.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How many dog breeds are in Heartopia?
faq.a1: There are 11 confirmed dog breeds: Beagle, Corgi, Golden Retriever, Labrador Retriever, Poodle, Rottweiler, Rural Dog, Shepherd Dog, Shiba Inu, Sled Dog, and Spotted Dog (Dalmatian). With all fur/eye/trait combinations, 200–500+ unique appearances are possible.
faq.q2: Do I need to adopt a cat before I can get a dog?
faq.a2: You must complete the Cat Caring tutorial with Mrs. Joan — but you don't need to actually adopt a cat. Finishing the tutorial unlocks Dog Caring as an available hobby.
faq.q3: Does breed affect gameplay stats or abilities?
faq.a3: No. All breeds share identical stats and can learn all the same tricks. Breed differences are purely visual. A Corgi and a Rottweiler perform identically in every gameplay mechanic.
faq.q4: How do I get a specific breed I want?
faq.a4: Check the adoption center every day. The rotation is fully random with no way to reroll — your desired breed will appear eventually. Rare breeds appear more frequently during seasonal events.
faq.q5: Can I have more than one dog?
faq.a5: Yes — up to 3 dogs total. The second slot unlocks at Dog Caring Level 4, and the third at Dog Caring Level 8.
faq.q6: Can I return or rehome a dog I don't want?
faq.a6: Currently, no. Dogs cannot be returned to the shelter. The only option is to board them for 20,000g, but they remain in your household permanently.
faq.q7: Do cats and dogs get along?
faq.a7: Yes! If you own both, they interact with friendly animations. Sociable pets initiate more cross-species interactions than Lone Wolf or Unsociable ones.

homeEvaluation.metaTitle: Heartopia Home Evaluation: Submit & Max Score
homeEvaluation.metaDesc: How to submit your home evaluation in Heartopia, how the scoring formula works, best furniture to raise your score fast, weekly strategy, and free vs. paid tips.
homeEvaluation.heroDesc: Everything you need to submit your home evaluation in Heartopia and maximize your score — the scoring formula, furniture priority, weekly reset strategy, and both free-to-play and paid routes. Updated February 2026.
homeEvaluation.quote: The scoring formula rewards diversity, not quantity. One new unique furniture piece is worth roughly 100× a duplicate — filling your home with varied types is always better than repeating the same piece.

what.title: What Is the Home Evaluation System?
what.intro: Home Evaluation is a weekly automated scoring system in Heartopia. After decorating your home, you submit it to the D.G. Member's Guild for assessment. The system scores your home based on furniture diversity, collectibles, plants, and building structure — then awards Gold, Wishing Stars, and other resources based on your score tier.
rewards: Gold coins based on score tier — a consistent passive income source
Wishing Stars for the gacha system
what.note: You can submit multiple times before the weekly cutoff (Friday night / Saturday). Only your highest score in that week counts.

how.title: How to Submit Your Home Evaluation — 4 Steps
how.intro: Submitting takes under 30 seconds. Here's the exact path:
how.step1: Tap the Watch icon in the top-right corner of your screen.
how.step2: In the side panel, locate and enter D.G. Member's Guild.
how.step3: Tap the Evaluation button in the top-right of the Guild screen.
how.step4: Tap Submit Evaluation — done! The system grades your home instantly.
how.tip: 💡 You can re-submit after adding new furniture to potentially improve your score. Aim to submit by Friday evening for maximum weekly benefit.
how.warning: The system scores your current home state at the moment of submission. Make sure all new furniture and collectibles are placed before you submit.

scoring.title: How the Scoring Formula Works
scoring.intro: The evaluation system does NOT judge interior design style or aesthetics. It runs a pure content-diversity algorithm:
scoring.rule1: First placement of any unique furniture / item type: ~100 points
scoring.rule2: Every additional duplicate of the same item: ~1 point (almost worthless)
scoring.rule3: Result: Variety is everything. 20 different items beats 100 copies of the same item.

categories.label: Furniture types
categories.desc: Each distinct furniture category (bed, sofa, desk, kitchen set, etc.) counted independently

categories.label: Plants & flowers
categories.desc: Both level and variety matter — higher-level flowers score more

categories.label: 5-star collectibles
categories.desc: High-star items from your Collection book

categories.label: Food items
categories.desc: Placed cooked dishes count as unique collectible types

categories.label: Building structure
categories.desc: Floors and architecture add points — but require real layout, not just stacked floors with no design

scoring.warn1: ❌ Style does not matter — the system is blind to whether your home looks "good".
scoring.warn2: ❌ More walls ≠ more points — structure is counted, but it needs actual living spaces.
scoring.warn3: ❌ One full furniture set repeated room after room = very low score.

boost.title: Best Ways to Increase Your Score
boost.furDesc: Every distinct furniture category adds ~100 points. Use furniture sets that span multiple categories (kitchen set, bedroom set, outdoor set). Never fill an extra room by duplicating furniture from another room — place new types instead.
boost.flowerDesc: Swapping from tulips/hibiscus to level 9–10 roses and lilies gave players ~1,000 extra points in community tests. High-level flowers are one of the best free-to-play score boosts.
boost.collectDesc: Complete hobby activities (fishing, bug catching, painting, etc.) to fill your Collection book with 5-star entries, then physically place the items in your home. Each new collectible type placed adds to your score.
boost.paidDesc: Premium/paid furniture items score significantly more per item than free furniture. If you spend on the game, prioritizing diverse premium furniture types will yield the biggest score jumps — especially mid-to-late game.
boost.foodDesc: Cooked dishes placed as decoration count as collectible types. Each dish type is unique — a full spread of different recipes adds meaningful points without spending Gold.
boost.structDesc: Multi-floor builds add points. However, an empty stacked structure with no furniture drops your score. Build floors with actual furniture and collectibles inside each space.

weekly.title: Weekly Score Strategy
weekly.intro: The score cap in Heartopia increases gradually each week with game updates. This means:
weekly.rule1: You cannot hit max score by grinding furniture in one week — the ceiling rises incrementally.
weekly.rule2: Each week, follow new game updates and place newly obtained furniture/collectibles before submitting.
weekly.rule3: Expected weekly gains: 600–1,000 points per week for active players who place all new items.

schedule.action: Acquire new furniture, collectibles, and flowers. Complete hobbies.
Place ALL new items in your home. Run a final check for uncollected items.
⭐ Submit evaluation for your best score of the week.
Submit again if you made further additions. Score resets after weekly cutoff.

weekly.tip: Don't wait for a "perfect" home — submit every week. A smaller score gain each week adds up faster than waiting months to submit one big batch.

ftp.title: Free-to-Play vs. Paid Player Strategy
freeItems: Complete all 6 available hobbies to maximize your collectible 5-star item count
Grow every flower variety and upgrade them to level 9–10
Cook every available recipe and place one of each as decoration
Expand home plots (up to 8) to fit more furniture diversity
Use furniture sets earned through Resident Requests and Guild rewards
Expect plateau around 60–70% of max score without paid items
paidItems: Focus on buying diverse furniture types rather than full matching room sets
Each new premium furniture category = +100–200 pts
Late-game score gap between paid and free-to-play players becomes significant
Premium seasonal event furniture often introduces new categories not available free
ftp.note: The evaluation system is designed to reward both routes — free players grow steadily through hobby completion and gardening, while paid players get bigger weekly jumps.

table.title: Score Priority Table
table.intro: Use this table to decide what to focus on each week:
table.note: ⚠️ These values are community-estimated based on player testing. Exact formula is not officially published.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Submit every Friday — even a +200 point improvement matters for weekly rewards.
Never place duplicate furniture in different rooms. Always choose a new furniture type.
After unlocking a new hobby, immediately convert its 5-star collectibles into placed items.
Flowers matter more than most players realize. Level-up your gardens before buying new furniture.
Check the evaluation feedback after each submission — the system lists the categories where you're "lacking", which is your exact to-do list.
If the feedback says "not enough collectibles", that's the hardest problem — focus on unlocking more hobbies to solve it long-term.
Building structure should look like an actual house, not a scoring farm — the system detects empty stacked rooms and penalizes them.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: When does the home evaluation reset?
faq.a1: The evaluation window resets weekly, in line with the game's weekly server reset (typically Friday night/Saturday). You can submit multiple times during the week and only your highest score counts.
faq.q2: Does decoration style affect the score?
faq.a2: No. The scoring system is purely algorithmic — it counts unique item types and ignores aesthetics. A chaotic room with 20 different furniture types outscores a beautiful room with the same sofa repeated 20 times.
faq.q3: Why is my score not increasing even after adding furniture?
faq.a3: If you're placing duplicate furniture types you already own, the score gain is only ~1 point each. Check if you already have that furniture category in your home. Also, the weekly score cap may have been reached — try again next week.
faq.q4: How many times can I submit per week?
faq.a4: You can submit as many times as you want during the weekly window. Only your highest score is recorded. Best practice: submit on Monday after any additions, and again on Friday with all final placements done.
faq.q5: Do I need to reach a D.G. Guild level to unlock Home Evaluation?
faq.a5: Home Evaluation becomes available once you have access to the D.G. Member's Guild app via your in-game watch. This unlocks early in the game. There is no specific minimum D.G. level requirement to submit.
faq.q6: What score do I need for maximum rewards?
faq.a6: The score cap increases gradually each week as the game updates. Currently, approximately 10 hobbies worth of collectibles plus diverse premium furniture are needed to approach maximum score. Most active free-to-play players reach 60–70% of max score.
faq.q7: Does the location of furniture matter (inside vs. outside)?
faq.a7: According to community testing, only furniture inside the home perimeter (enclosed by fences) was consistently counted in early versions. It's recommended to enclose all furniture within your plot boundary to be safe.

roamingOak.metaTitle: Heartopia Roaming Oak: Find Oak-Oak Every Day
roamingOak.metaDesc: How to find Oak-Oak every day in Heartopia — 5-min search checklist, unlock quest, all 8 crafting recipes with days-to-collect, and daily timber tips.
roamingOak.heroTitle: Heartopia Roaming Oak (Oak-Oak): Complete Daily Guide
roamingOak.heroDesc: Everything you need to find Oak-Oak and collect Roaming Oak Timber every day — a fast search routine, how the daily spawn works, the unlock quest, all crafting recipes with days-to-collect planning, and pro tips. Updated February 2026.
roamingOak.quote: Oak-Oak stays put until you free him — he never despawns during the day. But miss him today and tomorrow he moves. Check your property every day, especially the corners and behind buildings.

today.title: How to Find Oak-Oak Today — 5-Minute Checklist
today.intro: Oak-Oak spawns in a random spot on your property every day. He does NOT roam the whole map — start and finish your search on your own land.
checks: Walk all 4 fence lines / property edges first — the most common spawn
Check all garden beds, crop fields, and flower areas
Look near every fruit tree and decorative tree on your plot
Rotate camera and check behind every building and structure
If still missing: check just outside your fence line — he can spawn slightly beyond
today.timeNote: ⏱ Average search time: 2–5 minutes with a systematic perimeter-first route.
today.axeNote: 🪓 Equip your axe before you start searching — saves time once you find him.
today.discordNote: 💬 Check the official Heartopia Discord #oak-oak-location channel where players share today's coordinates in real time.
today.noFastNote: ⚠️ Do NOT use fast travel until you've collected your timber — fast travel can cause his spawn position to reset.

looks.title: What Oak-Oak Looks Like (Spot Him Fast)
looks.intro: Knowing his appearance saves time during your daily search.

items.label: Size
items.desc: About twice your character's height — easy to spot from a distance

items.label: Face
items.desc: Friendly carved expression on his trunk with glowing eyes

items.label: Top
items.desc: Lush green leaf canopy on top of his head

items.label: Body
items.desc: Brown bark trunk with branch-like arms

items.label: Root binding
items.desc: Thick tangled roots around his feet binding him to the ground — the key visual cue

items.label: Sparkle effect
items.desc: Subtle particle sparkle around him — visible from a distance even behind objects

looks.action: Walk up to him and use your axe on the roots. He'll thank you and you'll receive 3 Roaming Oak Timber.

unlock.title: How to Unlock Oak-Oak (Quest Chain)
unlock.intro: Oak-Oak doesn't appear until you complete a specific quest chain. You only need to do this once.
steps: Reach D.G. Member Level 6 by completing Daily Resident Requests. You'll receive a letter in your mailbox.
The letter is from Andrew (Mr. Moped). Meet him in the northeastern corner of the map near the forest.
Andrew starts the "Astralis in Forest" quest — a magical presence has appeared in the forest.
Follow the quest marker. You'll meet Ranger Cassie and then Patti, who reports a strange creature in the Spirit Pine Forest.
Find Oak-Oak in the Spirit Pine Forest using the tracking icon on your map.
Use your axe to cut the roots binding him. He'll explain his situation and become your daily companion.
unlock.note: ✅ After completing the quest, Oak-Oak will appear on your property every day automatically.
unlock.timeNote: ⏱ D.G. Level 6 takes approximately 12 days of consistent daily request completion.

spawn.title: How the Daily Spawn Works
spawn.intro: Understanding the spawn rules prevents missed days and wasted search time.

rules.title: Always on your property
rules.desc: Oak-Oak spawns on or immediately adjacent to your home plot — not anywhere on the global map.

rules.title: New location every day
rules.desc: After server reset (approx. 6:00 AM server time), he moves to a brand new random spot on your land.

rules.title: He never despawns
rules.desc: If you haven't found him, he's still there. He stays in place all day until you free him.

rules.title: No stacking on missed days
rules.desc: If you miss a day, you lose those 3 timber. Tomorrow he resets regardless — there is no catch-up mechanic.

rules.title: More plots = more area to search
rules.desc: Having all 8 home plots means a larger search area, but also means he has more spawn points to choose from.

spawn.tip: Make Oak-Oak your first task every day — right after daily login. Build a habit: open game → go find Oak-Oak → do other tasks.

craft.title: All Roaming Oak Timber Crafting Recipes
craft.intro: You earn 3 timber per day. Use this table to plan how many days you need to save for each item.

craft.note: ⚠️ Roaming Oak Timber cannot be purchased in shops. Oak-Oak is the ONLY source. Don't sell it.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Do the perimeter-first search route every day — walk fence lines before checking the interior. This covers the 2 most common spawn zones first.
The sparkle particle effect is your best long-range spotter. Train your eye to notice it while running — you can spot Oak-Oak 10+ tiles away.
Don't fast travel after login until timber is collected. Fast travel has a known chance of causing his spawn state to re-randomize.
Oak-Oak has different dialogue each day and season — talk to him after freeing for lore and occasional hints about his adventures.
If you're getting a new home plot, check around its edges immediately — Oak-Oak spawns near boundaries and the new area adds new hotspots.
The Oak Tree Swing (15 timber) is the best item for home evaluation score and community showcase — prioritize it once you have basics.
After completing the Life Log collection milestones for Roaming Oak Timber, the Gacha Machine unlocks bonus rewards — collect consistently.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Where does Oak-Oak spawn today?
faq.a1: Oak-Oak's location is random each day — there is no fixed "today's location". He always spawns on or just outside your home property. Check fence lines, garden beds, near trees, property corners, and behind buildings in that order.
faq.q2: What level do I need to unlock Oak-Oak?
faq.a2: You need D.G. Member Level 6. At Level 6, you'll receive a letter from Andrew (Mr. Moped) that starts the "Astralis in Forest" quest. Completing this quest unlocks Oak-Oak as a daily visitor to your property.
faq.q3: Does Oak-Oak roam around the whole map?
faq.a3: No — this is a common misconception. After completing his unlock quest (where you first find him in Spirit Pine Forest), Oak-Oak visits YOUR PROPERTY each day. He spawns somewhere on your land, not anywhere on the global map.
faq.q4: I can't find Oak-Oak anywhere — what should I do?
faq.a4: Do a full systematic search: walk your entire fence perimeter, check all garden areas, look near every tree, inspect all 4 corners, and rotate your camera to check behind every building. Also check just outside your fence line. Make sure you haven't accidentally already freed him earlier in the day.
faq.q5: What can I craft with Roaming Oak Timber?
faq.a5: There are 8 exclusive crafting recipes: Oak Welcome Sign (3 timber), Oak Planter Box (4), Oak Garden Trellis (5), Oak Garden Bench (6), Oak Fence Gate (8), Oak Picnic Table (10), Oak Garden Arch (12), and Oak Tree Swing (15). None of these can be crafted with regular timber.
faq.q6: Do missed days of Oak-Oak collection stack up?
faq.a6: No. If you miss a day, you simply lose those 3 timber. The next day Oak-Oak spawns fresh in a new location — there is no catch-up or bonus for previous missed days.
faq.q7: Can I get Roaming Oak Timber from any other source?
faq.a7: Oak-Oak is the only source of Roaming Oak Timber. It cannot be purchased from shops, traded, or obtained through any other method. This is why consistent daily collection matters.

animalTrough.metaTitle: Heartopia Animal Trough Locations: All 8 Animals
animalTrough.metaDesc: All 8 Heartopia animal trough locations — exact directions, favorite foods with sources, bond level rewards, weather bonuses, and daily feeding route tip.
animalTrough.heroTitle: Heartopia Animal Trough Locations: All 8 Animals & Favorite Foods
animalTrough.heroDesc: Complete guide to finding every animal trough in Heartopia, what to feed each animal, how bond levels work, weather bonuses, and the fastest daily feeding route. Includes the "penguin trough" explanation. Updated February 2026.
animalTrough.quote: Feed every animal their favorite food during their preferred weather and you'll max bond with all 8 in just a few weeks. The rewards — furniture, Wishing Stars, and daily gifts — are absolutely worth the daily lap.

clarify.title: Looking for the Penguin Trough? (Read This First)
clarify.intro: There is no penguin trough in Heartopia. If you searched "penguin trough", you're likely thinking of one of two different things:
clarify.item1: 🐧 Penguin Stove — a special cooking station available during the Winter Frost event, found at Massimo's bistro near the ice rink. Used for exclusive winter recipes (frosted pancakes, iced drinks, Aurora Banquet). This is a cooking appliance, not an animal feeding trough.
clarify.item2: 🍽️ Animal Troughs — the 8 wild animal feeding stations spread across the island. There is no penguin among the animals. The 8 animals are: Bunny, Fox, Alpaca, Sika Deer, Sea Otter, Capybara, Panda, and Ferret.
clarify.note: Scroll down for all 8 trough locations with exact map directions.

unlock.title: How to Unlock the Animal Care System
unlock.intro: Animal troughs are hidden until you unlock the Animal Care system. Here's how:
steps: Progress through the main story and raise your D.G. Member Level to unlock the Animal Care section in your Town Guide.
Speak with NPC Annie — she will explain the island wildlife and how the feeding system works.
Complete Annie's introductory quest: find and fill the Sika Deer trough in the Forest. Wait for the animals to eat.
All 8 troughs are now active. Once you find a trough, track it anytime from the Animals app on your in-game watch.
unlock.tip: 💡 Place a manual map marker on each trough the first time you find it — the Animals app shows bond level but the map marker helps with your daily route.

animalTrough.animalsTitle: All 8 Animal Trough Locations & Favorite Foods
animalTrough.animalsIntro: Each animal has 3 favorite foods that give 2× bond progress. Feed during preferred weather for an additional bonus.

animals.name: Bunny

animals.tip: The first trough you find in the tutorial. Very easy to reach from town center.

foodSources: Buy from Massimo after cooking unlock

animals.name: Alpaca

animals.name: Sika Deer

animals.tip: House Salad requires the cooking hobby. Use Branches or Lettuce until you unlock it.

animals.name: Sea Otter

animals.tip: All 3 favorite foods come from fishing — pair this trough with your daily fishing session.

animals.tip: All 3 favorites are farmable crops or bushes — easiest animal to feed consistently.

foodGuide.title: Food Sourcing Difficulty Guide
foodGuide.intro: Some favorite foods are much easier to get than others. Here's how to prioritize:
foodGuide.tierHard: ⚠️ Hard (high fishing level or cooking required)
easyItems: Weeds — pull from any crop field
Carrots / Strawberries / Corn / Apples / Blueberries / Raspberries — grow from Blanc's seeds
Common Shrimp / Oriental Shrimp — fish rivers & East Sea at any level
medItems: Bamboo — harvest in Forest (no level req)
Mussel — Suburban Lake in rain or rainbow, Fishing Lv3
European Perch — any river, any time, any level
Sea Bass — ocean, any time, any level
hardItems: Largemouth Bass — Forest Lake, sunny/rainbow, Fishing Lv4
Meat — buy from Massimo (requires cooking hobby unlock)
House Salad — cook with 2 vegetables (requires cooking hobby)
foodGuide.note: 💡 Universal Animal Food from the Pet Shop works for all animals but gives no favorite-food bonus. Use it only when you're out of preferred foods.

bond.title: Bond Level Rewards (Levels 1–10)
bond.intro: Each bond level unlocks more features. Feed favorite foods in preferred weather to level up faster.

tiers.rewards: Basic interaction (feed only) → Petting unlocked → Brushing unlocked
Animal leaves gifts at trough → More interaction types → Home visits begin → Gift frequency increases
Exclusive themed furniture unlocked (figurines, puzzles) → Maximum gift frequency → Wishing Stars reward → Special emotes

bond.tip: ⭐ You earn Wishing Stars for each bond rank-up. With 8 animals × 10 levels, consistently feeding all animals is one of the best passive Wishing Star sources in the game.

weather.title: Weather Bonus Quick Reference
weather.intro: Interacting with animals during their preferred weather gives extra bond progress. Plan feeding sessions around the daily forecast.
weather.tip: ⛅ On cloudy days without clear sun or rain, feed all animals anyway — you still get standard bond progress. Don't skip just because the weather isn't perfect.

route.title: Fastest Daily Feeding Route
route.intro: Hit all 8 troughs in under 10 minutes using a bike. This route minimizes backtracking:
steps: Start at Bunny (Western Town Suburbs) — easiest to reach from home
Fox (Windmill Flower Fields) — head west
Alpaca (Purple Light Beach bridge) — continue south-west
Sea Otter (Fishing Village) — head south to coast
Sika Deer (Forest Bus Stop) — head north into forest
Panda (Jump Puzzle area) — continue south-east in forest
Capybara (Onsen Mountain) — head west to mountain
Ferret (Rosy River bridge) — finish north-west near home lots
route.tip: 🚲 Unlock the bicycle as early as possible — it cuts the full trough lap from 15+ minutes to under 10. Use your watch's Animals app to confirm trough locations after the first visit.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is there a penguin trough in Heartopia?
faq.a1: No. There is no penguin animal in the wild animal trough system. If you're thinking of "penguin," you may be thinking of the Penguin Stove — a cooking appliance used during the Winter Frost event at Massimo's bistro. The 8 animals with troughs are: Bunny, Fox, Alpaca, Sika Deer, Sea Otter, Capybara, Panda, and Ferret.
faq.q2: How do I unlock animal troughs?
faq.a2: Progress through the main story to unlock the Animal Care section in your Town Guide, then speak with NPC Annie. She gives you a quest to find the Sika Deer trough. Once completed, all 8 troughs become active across the island.
faq.q3: What happens if I miss feeding an animal for a day?
faq.a3: Nothing bad happens — you just miss that day's bond progress. The troughs reset daily and there is no penalty or catch-up mechanic. Simply resume feeding the next day.
faq.q4: What is the fastest way to raise bond level?
faq.a4: Feed all 3 favorite foods (not just any food) every day, and do it during the animal's preferred weather (sun or rain). Favorite foods give 2× bond progress. Weather bonuses add on top of that. Also complete all available interactions each day (petting, brushing, games) as each interaction adds bond XP.
faq.a5: Yes. Once your bond level reaches the "Friendly" tier (around Level 4–7), animals will occasionally visit your home and leave gifts. The probability increases further as you reach Best Friends level.
faq.q6: What rewards do I get from maxing bond with all 8 animals?
faq.a6: Each animal unlocks exclusive themed furniture (figurines, puzzles, decorations) at higher bond levels. You also earn Wishing Stars for each rank-up, and animals will leave regular gift drops at their troughs. These are some of the best passive decoration rewards in the game.
faq.q7: Does Universal Animal Food count as a favorite food?
faq.a7: No. Universal Animal Food (from the Pet Shop) can be fed to all animals but does not trigger the 2× bond bonus that favorite foods provide. Use it as a backup when you're out of the animal's specific favorites.
